diagnosis_name: Acute Severe Viral Hepatitis (with risk of Acute Liver Failure / Hyperammonemia)

first_line_drugs.notes: IV infusion regimen: Loading 125 mg/kg over 1 hour, then 50 mg/kg over 4 hours, then 125 mg/kg over 19 hours; Day 2 onward: 150 mg/kg over 24 hours, repeat next 24 hours as required. Preparation: Add 1000 mg NAC (2 ampoules of 5 ml each) in 40 ml of 5% DEXTROSE; adjust total volume and rate as per weight-based dose and infusion duration
10 mg IM once daily for 3 days for coagulopathy correction
5 mg/kg/dose PO every 12 hours for 21 days for cholestasis and bile flow improvement
1 ml/kg/dose PO 2–3 times daily; titrate to achieve 2–3 soft stools/day for prevention/treatment of hepatic encephalopathy
250–500 mg/kg/day PO in 4 divided doses every 6 hours for hyperammonemia management
Maintenance IV fluids as per Holliday-Segar calculation; avoid fluid overload; add MVI as per standard pediatric dosing

second_line_drugs.notes: 10–15 mg/kg/day PO in 2 divided doses; use if persistent or worsening hepatic encephalopathy despite lactulose

investigations.name: BLOOD GLUCOSE
investigations.indication: Monitor for hypoglycemia in acute liver failure

investigations.name: SERUM AMMONIA
investigations.indication: Assess and monitor hyperammonemia and encephalopathy

investigations.name: PT / INR
investigations.indication: Assess severity of liver dysfunction and coagulopathy

investigations.name: LIVER FUNCTION TEST (LFT)
investigations.indication: Monitor disease progression and hepatic injury

investigations.name: RENAL FUNCTION TEST (RFT)
investigations.indication: Assess renal involvement and hepatorenal syndrome risk

counselling: Child has severe liver infection which can affect brain and blood clotting; close monitoring is required — बच्चे को गंभीर लिवर संक्रमण है जो दिमाग और खून के थक्कों को प्रभावित कर सकता है; नज़दीकी निगरानी ज़रूरी है
Strict medication adherence is essential; do not miss doses especially lactulose and NAC — दवाइयाँ समय पर लेना बहुत जरूरी है; खासकर लैक्टुलोज़ और NAC की खुराक न छोड़ें
Avoid outside food, ensure adequate carbohydrate intake and hydration — बाहर का खाना न दें, पर्याप्त कार्बोहाइड्रेट और पानी दें
Do NOT give PARACETAMOL, antiemetics, or any over-the-counter drugs without doctor advice — डॉक्टर की सलाह के बिना पैरासिटामोल, उल्टी की दवा या कोई भी दवा न दें
Immediate follow-up required if child becomes drowsy, vomits repeatedly, or stops feeding — अगर बच्चा सुस्त हो जाए, बार-बार उल्टी करे या दूध न पिए तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Altered sensorium or excessive sleepiness
Persistent vomiting
Bleeding from gums / nose
Seizures or abnormal movements
Reduced urine output
Worsening jaundice or abdominal distension
referral_criteria: Refer urgently to pediatric gastroenterologist or liver transplant center if INR >2, rising ammonia, worsening encephalopathy, or no improvement within 24–48 hours
hospitalisation_criteria: All cases of severe hepatitis with coagulopathy, encephalopathy, persistent vomiting, hypoglycemia, or rising bilirubin/ammonia require admission (preferably ICU monitoring)
notes: Acute severe viral hepatitis can progress to acute liver failure; early NAC improves outcomes even in non-paracetamol liver injury. Strict drug avoidance (especially hepatotoxic drugs) is critical.
guideline_changes: NAC recommended even in non-acetaminophen acute liver failure (updated global practice)
expected_course: Initial worsening may occur over 3–5 days; recovery may take 2–4 weeks; close monitoring required for early detection of liver failure
key_clinical_points: NAC improves survival even in viral hepatitis-related liver failure
Lactulose is essential to prevent hepatic encephalopathy
Avoid hepatotoxic drugs including PARACETAMOL
Monitor INR and ammonia closely – key prognostic markers

severity_assessment.mild: Jaundice with stable vitals, no coagulopathy
severity_assessment.moderate: Jaundice with mild coagulopathy and elevated enzymes
severity_assessment.severe: Coagulopathy (INR >1.5), encephalopathy, hypoglycemia, rising ammonia

counselling: Oral thrush is common and easily treatable. — मुंह का छाला आम है और आसानी से ठीक हो जाता है।
It is not due to poor care. — यह खराब देखभाल की वजह से नहीं होता।
Maintain proper hygiene of feeding equipment. — दूध पिलाने के बर्तन साफ़ रखें।
Complete full course of medication even if symptoms improve early. — लक्षण कम होने पर भी दवाई पूरी करें।
Consult doctor if baby develops fever, feeding refusal or spreading lesions. — बुखार, दूध न पीना, या छाले बढ़ना दिखे तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।
warning_signs: Feeding refusal or excessive crying during feeds — दूध पीने से मना करना या दूध पीते समय बहुत रोना
Spreading lesions beyond mouth — छाले मुंह से बाहर फैलना
Fever — बुखार
Poor weight gain — वज़न न बढ़ना
Persistent or recurring infection despite treatment — इलाज के बावजूद बार-बार होना
Rash in diaper area — डायपर वाली जगह पर दाने
expected_course: Oral thrush in neonates usually responds well to topical antifungal therapy (clotrimazole or nystatin) within 7-14 days. Treatment should continue for 48 hours after resolution of lesions. Recurrence is common if hygiene measures are not followed or if maternal nipple candidiasis is untreated.
key_clinical_points: White adherent plaques that do not wipe off easily are the hallmark of oral thrush.
Milk residue is easily wiped away and does not leave erythema underneath.
Clotrimazole 1% mouth paint or Nystatin oral suspension are first-line treatments.
Always treat concurrent maternal nipple candidiasis to prevent reinfection.
Sterilize all feeding equipment (bottles, nipples, pacifiers) daily.
Systemic fluconazole is reserved for refractory or systemic candidiasis under specialist guidance.

severity_assessment.mild: Few white patches, feeding unaffected, no systemic symptoms — topical antifungal and hygiene advice.
severity_assessment.moderate: Multiple patches, some feeding difficulty or irritability — topical antifungal, close follow-up.
severity_assessment.severe: Extensive lesions, feeding refusal, failure to thrive, systemic symptoms — consider systemic therapy, rule out immunodeficiency.

diagnosis_name: Hyperleukocytosis with / without Tumor Lysis Syndrome

first_line_drugs.notes: Aggressive hydration to maintain urine output and reduce blood viscosity / tumor lysis risk. In asymptomatic hyperleukocytosis: start 2 times maintenance IV fluids. In symptomatic hyperleukocytosis / TLC >2-3 lakhs/cc: start 3-4 times maintenance or approximately 3000 mL/m2/day. Monitor for fluid overload.
For tumor lysis prophylaxis in hyperleukocytosis / high uric acid risk. Dose as per institutional pediatric oncology protocol; start early with hydration.
Use in high-risk tumor lysis syndrome or significant hyperuricemia; preferred when uric acid is elevated or rapid control is needed.
Avoid routine PRBC transfusion before cytoreduction if hyperviscosity is present. Use platelet transfusion for severe thrombocytopenia / bleeding as clinically indicated.

second_line_drugs.notes: Use according to leukemia subtype and oncology protocol for leukoreduction if symptomatic hyperleukocytosis or rapidly rising counts are present.
Urgent leukapheresis or exchange transfusion may be considered in symptomatic hyperleukocytosis with leukostasis or exchange-transfusion-responsive situations.
Consider if tumor lysis syndrome causes refractory hyperkalemia, severe hyperphosphatemia, symptomatic hypocalcemia, fluid overload, or acute kidney injury.

investigations.name: Complete Blood Count with Differential and Platelets
investigations.indication: Diagnosis of hyperleukocytosis and baseline monitoring

investigations.name: Peripheral Smear / Bone Marrow / Flow Cytometry as indicated
investigations.indication: Confirm leukemia subtype and guide cytoreduction

investigations.name: Serum Uric Acid
investigations.indication: Tumor lysis syndrome diagnosis and monitoring

investigations.name: Serum Potassium, Phosphorus, Calcium, Creatinine, Urea
investigations.indication: Cairo-Bishop laboratory TLS assessment and AKI monitoring

investigations.name: Chest X-ray / Neuroimaging as clinically indicated

counselling: This is an oncologic emergency and needs urgent hospital treatment. — यह कैंसर से जुड़ी आपातकालीन स्थिति है और तुरंत अस्पताल में इलाज जरूरी है।

Very high white cell count can block blood flow and affect lungs, brain, kidneys, and heart. — बहुत ज्यादा सफेद रक्त कोशिकाएं खून के बहाव को रोक सकती हैं और फेफड़ों, दिमाग, किडनी और दिल को नुकसान पहुंचा सकती हैं।
Strong hydration and repeated blood tests are required to prevent tumor lysis complications. — ट्यूमर लाइसिस की जटिलताओं से बचने के लिए पर्याप्त पानी और बार-बार खून की जांच जरूरी है।
Breathlessness, drowsiness, seizures, reduced urine output, or chest symptoms must be reported immediately. — सांस फूलना, सुस्ती, दौरे, पेशाब कम होना या सीने में तकलीफ होने पर तुरंत बताएं।
Treatment may include cytoreduction, ICU care, and specialist oncology management. — इलाज में कोशिका कम करने की प्रक्रिया, ICU देखभाल और कैंसर विशेषज्ञ का इलाज शामिल हो सकता है।
warning_signs: Tachypnea or dyspnea
Headache, confusion, or altered sensorium
Seizures
Oliguria or anuria
Arrhythmia
Sudden weakness, visual symptoms, or coma
referral_criteria: Immediate referral to pediatric oncologist / intensivist for any hyperleukocytosis with symptoms of leukostasis, TLS, neurological signs, respiratory distress, arrhythmia, or renal dysfunction.
hospitalisation_criteria: All patients with hyperleukocytosis, suspected leukemia with TLC >100000/uL, clinical leukostasis, laboratory TLS, or need for IV hydration / cytoreduction / ICU monitoring require admission.
notes: Hyperleukocytosis is usually defined as WBC/TLC >100000/uL. Leukostasis symptoms may involve pulmonary, CNS, genitourinary, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular systems. Cairo-Bishop laboratory TLS is present if 2 or more occur within 3 days before or 7 days after therapy: uric acid >=8 mg/dL or 25% rise, potassium >=6 mEq/L or 25% rise, phosphorus >=6.5 mg/dL or 25% rise, calcium <=7 mg/dL or 25% fall. Clinical TLS includes creatinine >=1.5 times upper limit normal, cardiac arrhythmia / sudden death, or seizures.
source: Provided pediatric hyperleukocytosis and tumor lysis syndrome protocol
expected_course: Stabilization depends on early hydration, cytoreduction, and prevention of TLS. Lab abnormalities may worsen over the first few days of treatment and require frequent reassessment.
key_clinical_points: Hyperleukocytosis with leukostasis is an emergency requiring rapid hydration and oncology input.
Tumor lysis syndrome can occur before or after therapy and must be anticipated early.
Avoid unnecessary PRBC transfusion before leukoreduction in severe hyperviscosity unless clearly indicated.

severity_assessment.mild: High TLC without symptoms of leukostasis or TLS; hemodynamically stable.
severity_assessment.moderate: Hyperleukocytosis with rising uric acid / electrolytes or mild respiratory / neurological symptoms.
severity_assessment.severe: Symptomatic leukostasis, TLC often >2-3 lakhs/cc, CNS or pulmonary compromise, arrhythmia, AKI, or clinical tumor lysis syndrome.

monitoring_parameters.parameter: Uric acid, potassium, phosphorus, calcium, creatinine
Fluid balance and signs of overload

first_line_drugs.notes: Initial 4 hours IV fluids; bolus 10–20 ml/kg over 30–60 min only if shock; then continue deficit + maintenance over 36–48 hrs
Switch after initial 4 hours if available; continue rehydration over 36–48 hrs
Start after 1 hour of fluids at 0.05–0.1 U/kg/hr; no bolus; prepare 0.5–1 U/ml solution and infuse via pump
Add 40 mEq/L once urine output established and K+ normal; if hypokalemia use up to 80 mEq/L with ECG monitoring
Add when blood glucose <250 mg/dL; increase to D10 if glucose <150 mg/dL while acidosis persists

second_line_drugs.notes: Avoid routine use; consider only if severe acidosis (pH <6.9) with hemodynamic compromise
For suspected cerebral edema: Mannitol 0.5–1 g/kg or 3% saline 5 ml/kg IV

counselling: Child is critically ill and requires ICU care — बच्चा गंभीर रूप से बीमार है और ICU में इलाज जरूरी है
Insulin and fluids will correct the condition gradually — इंसुलिन और तरल पदार्थ से हालत धीरे-धीरे सुधरेगी
Frequent monitoring is required to prevent complications — जटिलताओं से बचने के लिए बार-बार जांच जरूरी है
Do not stop treatment early even if sugar improves — शुगर ठीक होने पर भी इलाज जल्दी बंद न करें
Long-term diabetes management will be needed — लंबे समय तक मधुमेह (डायबिटीज) का इलाज चलेगा
warning_signs: Altered consciousness
Seizures
Severe dehydration
Deep rapid breathing (Kussmaul breathing)
Headache or vomiting (cerebral edema)
Sudden drop in heart rate or BP
referral_criteria: Refer to PICU/endocrinologist if severe DKA, altered sensorium, or complications
hospitalisation_criteria: All DKA cases require admission; ICU if moderate–severe DKA
notes: Correct dehydration slowly over 36–48 hrs. Avoid rapid fluid correction to prevent cerebral edema. Do not give insulin bolus.
source: ISPAD Guidelines 2022/2024, IAP Pediatric Endocrine Guidelines
guideline_changes: Lower insulin dose (0.05 U/kg/hr) preferred in young children to reduce cerebral edema risk
expected_course: Improvement in 24–48 hrs; full correction of acidosis within 36–48 hrs
key_clinical_points: Fluid therapy is first priority before insulin
Never give insulin bolus in pediatric DKA
Watch closely for cerebral edema

severity_assessment.mild: pH 7.2–7.3, mild dehydration
severity_assessment.moderate: pH 7.1–7.2, moderate dehydration, tachypnea
severity_assessment.severe: pH <7.1, severe dehydration, altered sensorium

first_line_drugs.notes: Neonates: 2 ml/kg IV bolus (200 mg/kg), then start infusion
Children: 2–4 ml/kg IV bolus (max D25 preferred), repeat if needed after 5–10 min
Start GIR 4–6 mg/kg/min, titrate up to 8–12 mg/kg/min (up to 20 mg/kg/min in hyperinsulinism)

second_line_drugs.notes: 0.03–0.1 mg/kg IM/IV (max 1 mg) in refractory hypoglycemia or no IV access
2–5 mg/kg IV if adrenal insufficiency suspected

counselling: Child needs urgent glucose correction and monitoring — बच्चे को तुरंत शुगर (ग्लूकोज़) ठीक करना और निगरानी ज़रूरी है।
Ensure frequent feeding after stabilization — स्थिति ठीक होने के बाद बार-बार थोड़ा-थोड़ा दूध/खाना दें।
Avoid prolonged fasting — बच्चे को लंबे समय तक भूखा न रखें।
Give medicines as advised strictly — दवाइयाँ समय पर और बताई गई मात्रा में दें।
Follow-up for cause evaluation is important — कारण की जाँच के लिए फॉलो-अप ज़रूरी है।
warning_signs: Seizures
Lethargy or unconsciousness
Poor feeding
Apnea or irregular breathing
Recurrent hypoglycemia
Abnormal movements
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/endocrinologist if persistent or recurrent hypoglycemia
hospitalisation_criteria: Symptomatic hypoglycemia, seizures, need for IV glucose infusion, recurrent episodes
notes: Always treat hypoglycemia immediately before confirming cause. Avoid delays.
source: IAP Guidelines 2024, WHO Emergency Care, Pediatric Endocrine Protocols
guideline_changes: Higher GIR targets recommended in hyperinsulinemic states
expected_course: Rapid improvement within minutes after correction; recurrence depends on underlying cause
key_clinical_points: Hypoglycemia is a medical emergency
Check glucose in all sick children
Start treatment immediately without waiting for reports

severity_assessment.mild: Asymptomatic low glucose
severity_assessment.moderate: Irritability, sweating, poor feeding
severity_assessment.severe: Seizures, coma, apnea

first_line_drugs.notes: For symptomatic hypocalcemia: 1 mL/kg/dose IV (provides about 9-18 mg/kg elemental calcium), maximum 10 mL, dilute with equal volume of D5W or distilled water and infuse slowly over 10 minutes under ECG monitoring
For recurrent symptomatic hypocalcemia: continuous infusion starting at 10-30 mg/kg/hour elemental calcium equivalent, titrate based on serial calcium levels and symptoms; ECG monitoring mandatory
For asymptomatic hypocalcemia or after stabilization: 100-200 mg/kg/day in 2-3 divided doses
For asymptomatic hypocalcemia/supportive therapy: 400-800 IU/day orally

second_line_drugs.notes: If hypomagnesemia is present or hypocalcemia is refractory: 25-50 mg/kg slow IV infusion; approximately 0.05-0.1 mL/kg of 50% solution; may repeat every 6 hours for 2-3 doses
Use in hypoparathyroidism: 20-60 ng/kg/day orally

investigations.indication: Diagnosis and serial monitoring of hypocalcemia

investigations.name: Ionized Calcium
investigations.indication: Best indicator in symptomatic child or critical illness

investigations.name: ECG
investigations.indication: Symptomatic hypocalcemia, arrhythmia, prolonged QT, or IV calcium therapy

investigations.name: Serum Magnesium
investigations.indication: Refractory hypocalcemia or suspected combined deficiency

investigations.name: Serum Phosphate, Alkaline Phosphatase, Vitamin D, PTH
investigations.indication: Etiology workup for persistent or recurrent hypocalcemia

counselling: Low calcium can cause twitching, spasms, seizures, or heart rhythm changes. — कम कैल्शियम से मांसपेशियों में ऐंठन, झटके, दौरे या दिल की धड़कन बिगड़ सकती है।
Symptomatic low calcium needs urgent treatment and monitoring. — लक्षण वाले कम कैल्शियम का तुरंत इलाज और निगरानी ज़रूरी है।
After stabilization, oral calcium and vitamin D may be needed for several days or longer. — स्थिति ठीक होने के बाद भी कुछ दिनों तक मुँह से कैल्शियम और विटामिन डी लेना पड़ सकता है।
Regular blood tests are important to ensure calcium becomes normal and stays normal. — नियमित ब्लड टेस्ट कराएं ताकि कैल्शियम सामान्य बना रहे।
Seek urgent care if the child develops twitching, stridor, seizures, or poor feeding. — अगर बच्चे को झटके, साँस में आवाज़, दौरे या दूध न पीना हो तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।
warning_signs: Tetany or muscle twitching
Carpopedal spasm
Laryngospasm or noisy breathing
Seizures
Arrhythmia or palpitations
Excessive irritability or altered sensorium
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/endocrinologist if symptomatic hypocalcemia, recurrent episodes, arrhythmia, seizures, suspected hypoparathyroidism, or refractory hypocalcemia.
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if symptomatic hypocalcemia, seizures, laryngospasm, prolonged QT, arrhythmia, ionized calcium significantly low, or need for IV calcium infusion.
notes: Corrected calcium formula: Corrected calcium = Serum calcium + 0.8 x (4.0 - serum albumin). Symptoms usually occur when ionized calcium falls below about 0.8-0.9 mmol/L. ECG monitoring is required during IV calcium administration.
expected_course: Symptoms often improve quickly after IV calcium, while biochemical normalization may require continued oral calcium and vitamin D with repeat monitoring over days to weeks depending on cause.
key_clinical_points: Symptomatic hypocalcemia is a medical emergency.
Always check magnesium in refractory or recurrent hypocalcemia.
Continuous ECG monitoring is essential during IV calcium infusion.

severity_assessment.mild: Low serum calcium without symptoms; child stable and tolerating oral therapy.
severity_assessment.moderate: Low calcium with irritability, mild twitching, or biochemical abnormality needing close monitoring.
severity_assessment.severe: Tetany, carpopedal spasm, laryngospasm, seizures, prolonged QT, or arrhythmia.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: 6-12 hourly during acute correction, then daily once stable
Repeat as indicated in refractory cases
Hourly if laryngospasm or severe symptoms

first_line_drugs.notes: Moderate to severe hypercalcemia: start hydration to correct volume depletion; for severe hypercalcemia give 10-20 mL/kg IV bolus over 1 hour, then continue IV saline as needed (often 2-3 times maintenance initially depending on hydration and cardiac status)
1-2 mg/kg IV after adequate hydration to enhance calciuresis; avoid before volume status is corrected
0.5-2 mg/kg diluted in 30 mL normal saline, IV over 4 hours; use in severe or persistent hypercalcemia
2-4 units/kg subcutaneous every 12 hours for severe symptomatic hypercalcemia requiring faster calcium reduction

second_line_drugs.notes: 5 mg/kg orally twice daily as a bisphosphonate option where appropriate
2 mg/kg IV every 6 hours when indicated, especially where reduced intestinal calcium absorption is desired or steroid-responsive cause suspected

investigations.name: Serum Total Calcium
investigations.indication: Diagnosis and serial monitoring of severity

investigations.name: Renal Function Tests
investigations.indication: Assess dehydration, renal injury, and treatment safety

investigations.name: Serum Electrolytes (Na, K, Mg, Phosphate)
investigations.indication: Associated electrolyte abnormalities and monitoring during therapy

investigations.name: ECG
investigations.indication: Symptomatic hypercalcemia, arrhythmia, or severe hypercalcemia

investigations.name: PTH, Vitamin D, Urinary Calcium
investigations.indication: Etiology workup in persistent or recurrent hypercalcemia

counselling: High calcium can cause vomiting, constipation, excessive urine, irritability, and weakness. — ज्यादा कैल्शियम से उल्टी, कब्ज, ज्यादा पेशाब, चिड़चिड़ापन और कमजोरी हो सकती है।
Mild cases may only need fluids and removal of triggering medicines or excess calcium intake. — हल्के मामलों में सिर्फ पानी पिलाना और कैल्शियम बढ़ाने वाली दवाएं बंद करना काफी हो सकता है।
Severe cases need admission, IV fluids, and close monitoring. — गंभीर मामलों में भर्ती, IV तरल पदार्थ और करीबी निगरानी जरूरी है।
Avoid thiazide diuretics, lithium, and unnecessary calcium supplements unless specifically advised. — डॉक्टर की सलाह के बिना थायज़ाइड, लिथियम या कैल्शियम सप्लीमेंट न दें।
Follow-up blood tests are important because calcium may rise again depending on the cause. — फॉलो-अप खून की जांच जरूरी है क्योंकि कारण के अनुसार कैल्शियम फिर बढ़ सकता है।
warning_signs: Persistent vomiting
Dehydration or poor feeding
Marked irritability or lethargy
Polyuria / reduced urine output after dehydration
Constipation with abdominal distension
Arrhythmia or altered sensorium
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric endocrinologist/nephrologist/intensivist if calcium is >14 mg/dL, symptoms are significant, renal dysfunction is present, medical therapy fails, or recurrent/unclear etiology is suspected.
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if total serum calcium >14 mg/dL, symptomatic moderate hypercalcemia, dehydration, poor oral intake, arrhythmia, altered sensorium, or need for IV therapy/serial monitoring.
notes: Mild hypercalcemia (10-12 mg/dL) usually needs no specific drug therapy beyond hydration and avoiding offending agents. Moderate hypercalcemia (12-14 mg/dL) may not need immediate drug treatment unless symptomatic. Severe hypercalcemia (>14 mg/dL) requires urgent stepwise treatment.
expected_course: Symptoms improve as hydration is corrected and serum calcium falls. Mild cases may normalize with conservative care, while severe cases may take 24-72 hours or longer depending on cause and response to bisphosphonate therapy.
key_clinical_points: Hydration is the first step in treatment of clinically significant hypercalcemia.
Furosemide should be used only after correcting volume depletion.
Persistent or severe hypercalcemia may require bisphosphonate, calcitonin, steroid, or dialysis-based management.

severity_assessment.mild: Total serum calcium 10-12 mg/dL, usually minimally symptomatic or asymptomatic.
severity_assessment.moderate: Total serum calcium 12-14 mg/dL, may have nausea, vomiting, constipation, polyuria, irritability, or fatigue.
severity_assessment.severe: Total serum calcium >14 mg/dL or significant symptoms such as dehydration, neurological changes, arrhythmia, or poor feeding.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: 6-12 hourly initially, then daily once improving

first_line_drugs.notes: Plan A: <2 yrs: 50–100 ml after each loose stool; 2–10 yrs: 100–200 ml; >10 yrs: as tolerated
Plan B: 75 ml/kg over 4 hours (for some dehydration), reassess after 4 hrs
Plan C: 100 ml/kg IV (30 ml/kg rapid then 70 ml/kg over 2.5–5 hrs depending on age)
<6 months: 10 mg OD for 14 days; >6 months: 20 mg OD for 14 days

second_line_drugs.notes: 0.15 mg/kg/dose PO/IV if persistent vomiting interfering with ORS
Optional; may reduce duration of diarrhea

counselling: Give ORS frequently in small amounts — ORS बार-बार थोड़ी-थोड़ी मात्रा में पिलाएं।
Continue breastfeeding and normal diet — माँ का दूध और सामान्य खाना जारी रखें।
Avoid sugary drinks and packaged juices — मीठे पेय और पैकेट वाले जूस न दें।
Maintain hand hygiene to prevent spread — हाथों की सफ़ाई रखें ताकि बीमारी न फैले।
Follow-up if no improvement in 2–3 days — 2-3 दिन में सुधार न हो तो दोबारा डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।
warning_signs: Lethargy or unconsciousness
Sunken eyes and poor drinking
Persistent vomiting
No urine for >6 hours
Blood in stool
Severe weakness or shock signs
referral_criteria: Refer if severe dehydration, shock, persistent vomiting, or no improvement after ORS therapy
hospitalisation_criteria: Severe dehydration, inability to drink, repeated vomiting, shock, altered sensorium
notes: ORS is cornerstone therapy. Avoid unnecessary antibiotics unless dysentery or specific infection suspected.
guideline_changes: Low osmolar ORS recommended universally; early feeding emphasized
expected_course: Diarrhea improves in 3–5 days; hydration improves within 24–48 hours
key_clinical_points: ORS is lifesaving and first-line treatment
Assess dehydration clinically before treatment
Reassess after 4 hours in Plan B

severity_assessment.mild: No signs of dehydration, normal drinking
severity_assessment.moderate: Restless, sunken eyes, thirsty, reduced skin turgor
severity_assessment.severe: Lethargic, unable to drink, weak pulse, delayed capillary refill

first_line_drugs.notes: Correct slowly over 24-84 hours depending on initial serum sodium. Use calculated fluid plan: Total fluid deficit + free water deficit + maintenance sodium. Avoid rapid correction.
Use first if child is in shock to restore intravascular volume before hypernatremia correction. Bolus as per shock protocol.

first_line_drugs.drug: Half Normal Saline based dextrose fluid (e.g., N/2, N/5, N/6 with dextrose as per calculated sodium requirement)
first_line_drugs.notes: Choose fluid strength according to calculated sodium requirement and day-wise correction plan; usual target sodium fall not more than 10-12 mEq/L in 24 hours or about 0.5 mEq/L/hr.

second_line_drugs.notes: Only if seizures occur during correction due to overly rapid sodium fall or cerebral edema from hypotonic correction; use to acutely raise serum sodium under ICU monitoring.
Add only after urine output is established and potassium status is known, if clinically indicated.

investigations.name: Serum Sodium
investigations.indication: Diagnosis and serial monitoring during correction

investigations.name: Serum Potassium, Chloride, Bicarbonate
investigations.indication: Baseline electrolyte assessment and monitoring during IV correction

investigations.name: Blood Glucose
investigations.indication: Baseline metabolic assessment and before selecting dextrose-containing fluids

investigations.name: Renal Function Tests
investigations.indication: Assess dehydration severity, renal perfusion, and ongoing monitoring

investigations.name: Urine output monitoring
investigations.indication: Guide fluid therapy and assess renal perfusion

investigations.name: Weight
investigations.indication: Needed for calculating free water deficit and maintenance fluids

counselling: High sodium is usually due to water loss more than salt loss and needs slow correction. — ज्यादा सोडियम आमतौर पर शरीर में पानी की कमी से होता है और धीरे-धीरे ठीक करना जरूरी है।
Do not give plain water or change IV fluids abruptly without medical advice. — डॉक्टर की सलाह के बिना सादा पानी न दें या IV तरल पदार्थ अचानक न बदलें।
Treatment is usually gradual over 1 to 3 days depending on sodium level. — इलाज आमतौर पर 1 से 3 दिन में धीरे-धीरे किया जाता है।
Strict monitoring of urine output, sensorium, and repeat sodium tests is essential. — पेशाब, होश और सोडियम जांच की सख्त निगरानी जरूरी है।
Come back immediately or alert staff if the child becomes drowsy, irritable, or has seizures. — अगर बच्चा सुस्त, चिड़चिड़ा हो जाए या दौरे पड़ें तो तुरंत स्टाफ को बताएं।
warning_signs: Seizures
Altered sensorium or unusual drowsiness
Marked irritability or lethargy
Poor perfusion / shock
Persistent vomiting
Reduced urine output or inability to drink
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/nephrologist if serum sodium is markedly elevated, correction is complex, there is shock, neurological symptoms, persistent sodium rise, or associated renal dysfunction.
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if serum sodium >145 mEq/L with dehydration needing IV fluids, neurological symptoms, shock, inability to tolerate oral fluids, or need for serial biochemical monitoring.
notes: Hypernatremia correction must be slow. If child is in shock, restore circulation first with isotonic fluid. Acute hypernatremia can be corrected over 24-36 hours; chronic hypernatremia usually over 48 hours or longer. Rapid reduction can cause cerebral edema.
expected_course: Serum sodium should fall gradually, generally not more than 10-12 mEq/L per 24 hours. Clinical improvement parallels rehydration and normalization of sensorium over 24-84 hours depending on severity.
key_clinical_points: Treat shock first with isotonic saline before starting planned correction.
Target sodium fall should not exceed about 0.5 mEq/L/hr.
Seizures during correction suggest overly rapid sodium fall and need urgent reassessment.

severity_assessment.mild: Serum sodium 146-150 mEq/L, mild thirst or dehydration, stable vitals.
severity_assessment.moderate: Serum sodium 151-169 mEq/L with clinical dehydration, irritability, poor feeding, or reduced urine output.
severity_assessment.severe: Serum sodium >=170 mEq/L, shock, neurological symptoms, seizures, or significant dehydration.

first_line_drugs.notes: Symptomatic (Na <125 with seizures/encephalopathy): 4–6 ml/kg IV over 30 min; may repeat 2 ml/kg over 15 min if seizures persist; target rise 5–10 mEq/L
Asymptomatic (Na <125): correct slowly using calculated Na deficit over 4–24 hrs
50–75% of maintenance fluids in SIADH-related hyponatremia

second_line_drugs.notes: Used in hypovolemic hyponatremia to restore volume
1 mg/kg IV in selected cases (SIADH with fluid overload)

counselling: Low sodium levels can affect brain function — सोडियम कम होने से दिमाग की कार्यप्रणाली प्रभावित हो सकती है।
Treatment will be done slowly to avoid complications — इलाज धीरे-धीरे किया जाएगा ताकि कोई दिक्कत न हो।
Fluid intake may be restricted as advised — डॉक्टर की सलाह अनुसार पानी/तरल पदार्थ सीमित रखें।
Regular blood tests are required for monitoring — निगरानी के लिए नियमित ब्लड टेस्ट ज़रूरी हैं।
Follow-up is important to identify underlying cause — असली कारण पता लगाने के लिए फॉलो-अप ज़रूरी है।
warning_signs: Seizures
Altered consciousness
Vomiting
Headache
Apnea
Worsening lethargy
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist if symptomatic hyponatremia or rapid sodium fall
hospitalisation_criteria: Na <125 mEq/L, neurological symptoms, seizures, or need for IV correction
notes: Avoid rapid correction. Increase Na should not exceed 10–12 mEq/L/day or 0.5 mEq/L/hr to prevent osmotic demyelination.
source: IAP Guidelines 2024, Pediatric Electrolyte Management Protocols
guideline_changes: Emphasis on controlled correction rate and early use of 3% saline in symptomatic cases
expected_course: Improvement within 24–48 hrs with proper correction
key_clinical_points: Treat symptoms first (especially seizures)
Correct sodium slowly
Always identify and treat underlying cause (e.g., SIADH)

severity_assessment.mild: Na 130–134 mEq/L, no symptoms
severity_assessment.moderate: Na 125–129 mEq/L, mild symptoms (nausea, irritability)
severity_assessment.severe: Na <125 mEq/L with seizures, coma, encephalopathy

diagnosis_name: Acid-Base Disorder (Based on Arterial Blood Gas Analysis)

first_line_drugs.notes: Administer to maintain SpO2 >94% in respiratory disorders or hypoxia
For dehydration, shock, or metabolic acidosis due to fluid loss; 10-20 mL/kg bolus as needed
Indicated in severe metabolic acidosis (pH <7.1); dose 1-2 mEq/kg IV slowly after ABG confirmation

second_line_drugs.notes: For respiratory failure or severe acidosis/alkalosis not responding to medical therapy

second_line_drugs.drug: Treat underlying cause (e.g., antibiotics, insulin, bronchodilators)
second_line_drugs.notes: Definitive management depends on primary disorder identified in ABG

investigations.name: Serum Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl, HCO3)
investigations.indication: Anion gap calculation and cause identification

counselling: ABG helps identify the type of acid-base imbalance in the body. — ABG शरीर में एसिड-बेस असंतुलन का प्रकार पहचानने में मदद करता है।
Treatment depends on the underlying cause rather than just correcting numbers. — इलाज सिर्फ नंबर ठीक करने से नहीं, बल्कि मूल कारण ठीक करने पर निर्भर करता है।
Frequent monitoring is required in critically ill patients. — गंभीर रूप से बीमार बच्चों में बार-बार जांच जरूरी है।
Do not delay treatment in severe cases while awaiting reports. — गंभीर मामलों में रिपोर्ट का इंतजार किए बिना इलाज शुरू करें।
Follow-up tests are important to ensure recovery. — ठीक होने की पुष्टि के लिए फॉलो-अप जांच जरूरी है।
warning_signs: Rapid breathing or respiratory distress
Altered consciousness
Seizures
Shock or poor perfusion
Severe dehydration
Cardiac arrhythmias
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist if severe acid-base imbalance, shock, respiratory failure, or mixed disorder suspected
hospitalisation_criteria: Severe metabolic acidosis/alkalosis, respiratory failure, altered sensorium, or need for IV therapy/ventilation
notes: Always follow systematic approach: assess pH → identify primary disorder → calculate compensation → check anion gap → evaluate delta ratio for mixed disorders.
expected_course: Improvement depends on correction of underlying cause; ABG normalizes gradually with treatment
key_clinical_points: pH determines acidosis vs alkalosis
Always identify primary disorder before treating
Anion gap helps differentiate metabolic acidosis causes

severity_assessment.mild: Slight pH abnormality with stable vitals
severity_assessment.moderate: Abnormal pH with clinical symptoms
severity_assessment.severe: pH <7.1 or >7.6 with life-threatening symptoms

diagnosis_name: Metabolic Acidosis / Indications for Sodium Bicarbonate Therapy

first_line_drugs.notes: Indicated in selected severe metabolic acidosis: pH <7.1 and/or bicarbonate <8-10 mEq/L and/or base excess >10 mmol/L. Dose based on HCO3 deficit: HCO3 needed (mEq) = 0.3 x weight (kg) x base deficit (or 0.3 x weight x (24 - serum HCO3)). Give half of calculated dose over 60 minutes, then reassess blood gases.

Prepare by mixing sodium bicarbonate solution with equal volume of sterile water or compatible solution. Administer as slow IV infusion over 30-60 minutes. In emergency, may be given as bolus 1 mEq/kg.

second_line_drugs.notes: Examples: shock correction, sepsis treatment, insulin for DKA, antidote/toxin management, ventilation support, renal support depending on etiology.

investigations.name: Arterial / Venous Blood Gas
investigations.indication: Confirm severe metabolic acidosis and monitor response to bicarbonate

investigations.name: Serum Electrolytes including HCO3, Na, K, Cl
investigations.indication: Assess severity, base deficit, associated electrolyte abnormalities

investigations.name: Renal Function Tests
investigations.indication: Assess renal cause or consequence of acidosis

investigations.name: Lactate / Anion Gap / Cause-specific tests
investigations.indication: Evaluate high anion gap metabolic acidosis and underlying etiology

counselling: Sodium bicarbonate is not used routinely in all acidosis cases. — सोडियम बाइकार्बोनेट हर एसिडोसिस में नहीं दिया जाता।
It is reserved for selected severe situations after doctor assessment. — यह केवल गंभीर स्थितियों में डॉक्टर की जांच के बाद दिया जाता है।
Treatment of the underlying cause is more important than bicarbonate alone. — मूल कारण का इलाज बाइकार्बोनेट से ज्यादा जरूरी है।
Frequent blood gas and electrolyte monitoring is needed during therapy. — इलाज के दौरान बार-बार ब्लड गैस और इलेक्ट्रोलाइट जांच जरूरी है।
Inform staff immediately if breathing worsens, irritability increases, or seizures occur. — सांस बिगड़ने, चिड़चिड़ापन बढ़ने या दौरे पड़ने पर तुरंत स्टाफ को बताएं।
warning_signs: Severe fast or labored breathing
Shock or poor perfusion
Altered sensorium
Cardiac dysfunction
Seizures
Worsening acidosis on repeat blood gas
referral_criteria: Refer to PICU/intensivist if severe metabolic acidosis, high anion gap acidosis with hemodynamic compromise, cardiopulmonary resuscitation setting, toxin-related acidosis, or refractory acidosis.
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if pH <7.2, bicarbonate markedly low, shock, altered sensorium, need for IV bicarbonate, suspected toxin/metabolic disease, or need for serial blood gas monitoring.
notes: Routine use in older children is not recommended unless specific indications are present. Common indications include severe normal anion gap acidosis, selected severe high anion gap acidosis, hyperkalemia, TCA poisoning, beta-blocker poisoning, and some peri-resuscitation situations. Ensure adequate ventilation before giving bicarbonate.
source: Provided sodium bicarbonate indication and administration protocol
expected_course: pH and bicarbonate improve gradually after infusion if the underlying cause is also corrected. Repeat reassessment is required within 1-2 hours or sooner in unstable patients.
key_clinical_points: Do not use sodium bicarbonate routinely in all acidosis.
Correct severe acidosis only in selected indications and reassess with blood gas.
Ensure IV line patency, compatibility, and adequate ventilation before administration.

severity_assessment.mild: Mild acidosis without hemodynamic compromise; usually treat underlying cause only.
severity_assessment.moderate: Biochemical acidosis with clinical illness but no clear bicarbonate indication yet.
severity_assessment.severe: pH <7.1 and/or HCO3 <8-10 mEq/L, significant base deficit, shock, cardiac dysfunction, or selected toxic/metabolic indication.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: After first infusion and then 1-2 hourly as needed

first_line_drugs.notes: 100 mg/kg/dose IV (approximately 1 mL/kg/dose) over 5-10 minutes for ECG changes or severe hyperkalemia; membrane stabilizing; onset 1-3 min; duration about 30 min
1-2 mEq/kg/dose IV over 5-10 minutes; especially useful if associated acidosis; onset 10-30 min; duration about 2 hours
Glucose 0.5 g/kg (approximately 2 mL/kg of 25% dextrose) plus regular insulin 0.1 U/kg IV over 15-30 minutes; shifts potassium intracellularly; onset about 30 min; duration 2-4 hours
2.5 mg if <25 kg, 5 mg if >25 kg, nebulized over 10 minutes; promotes intracellular shift of potassium; onset about 120 min

second_line_drugs.notes: 0.5-1 g orally or rectally; enhances gastrointestinal potassium elimination; onset 60-120 min; duration 4-6 hours
1-2 mg/kg IV if urine output is adequate; enhances urinary potassium excretion; onset 15-30 min; duration 4-6 hours

investigations.name: 12-lead ECG
investigations.indication: All suspected or confirmed hyperkalemia; assess ECG changes and severity

investigations.name: Serum Potassium
investigations.indication: Diagnosis and serial monitoring during treatment

investigations.name: Renal Function Tests
investigations.indication: Assess renal cause and need for dialysis

investigations.name: Urine Output Monitoring
investigations.indication: Guide diuretic use and assess renal perfusion

counselling: High potassium can affect the heart and may become life-threatening. — ज्यादा पोटैशियम दिल को प्रभावित कर सकता है और जानलेवा हो सकता है।
Continuous monitoring is needed until potassium becomes safe. — पोटैशियम सामान्य होने तक लगातार निगरानी जरूरी है।
All potassium-containing oral feeds, IV fluids, and medicines should be reviewed and stopped if advised. — पोटैशियम वाले खाने, तरल पदार्थ और दवाइयाँ बंद करें जैसा डॉक्टर कहें।
Do not use over-the-counter supplements or salt substitutes unless approved. — डॉक्टर की मंजूरी के बिना कोई सप्लीमेंट या नमक का विकल्प न लें।
Seek urgent care if weakness, palpitations, or reduced urine output develops. — कमजोरी, धड़कन बढ़ना या पेशाब कम होने पर तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।
warning_signs: Muscle weakness
Paresthesia
Palpitations
Bradycardia
Arrhythmia
Reduced urine output
referral_criteria: Refer urgently to pediatric intensivist/nephrologist if ECG changes are present, serum potassium is markedly elevated, medical treatment fails, renal failure is suspected, or dialysis may be required.
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if serum potassium >5.5 mEq/L with symptoms, ECG changes, renal dysfunction, ongoing potassium load, or need for IV therapy/cardiac monitoring.
notes: Obtain ECG and place on cardiac monitor immediately. Eliminate potassium from diet and parenteral fluids. Stop drugs that can worsen hyperkalemia, such as ACE inhibitors and beta blockers. Dialysis is recommended if medical measures are unsuccessful.
expected_course: Cardiac stabilization is immediate after calcium therapy, while potassium shift and elimination occur over minutes to hours. Serial reassessment is required until potassium normalizes and ECG changes resolve.
key_clinical_points: Calcium stabilizes myocardium but does not lower total body potassium.
Insulin with glucose, bicarbonate, and salbutamol shift potassium intracellularly.
Definitive potassium removal may require resin, diuresis, or dialysis.

severity_assessment.mild: Serum K+ 5.5-6.5 mEq/L; may show tall peaked T waves and prolonged PR interval.
severity_assessment.moderate: Serum K+ 6.5-8 mEq/L; may show loss of P waves, QRS widening, ST changes, ectopic beats, or escape rhythm.
severity_assessment.severe: Serum K+ >8 mEq/L or any level with severe ECG changes such as sine wave pattern, AV block, ventricular arrhythmias, bradycardia, or asystole.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: 30-60 min after insulin, then as needed

first_line_drugs.notes: For severe hypokalemia (serum K+ <2.5 mEq/L) or hypokalemia with ECG changes: prepare stock solution with 90 mL Normal Saline + 10 mL KCl; infuse at 1.5-2.5 mL/kg/hour to deliver 0.3-0.5 mEq/kg/hour under strict continuous ECG monitoring; continue until serum K+ is at least 3 mEq/L or ECG changes normalize
For mild to moderate hypokalemia without ECG changes and when oral route is tolerated; dose as per potassium deficit and repeat serum potassium monitoring

second_line_drugs.notes: Use in refractory hypokalemia or suspected magnesium deficiency: 25-50 mg/kg slow IV infusion; approximate volume 0.05-0.1 mL/kg of 50% solution; may repeat every 6 hours for total 2-3 doses

investigations.name: Serum Potassium
investigations.indication: Diagnosis and repeat monitoring during correction

investigations.name: ECG
investigations.indication: Severe hypokalemia, symptoms, or suspected arrhythmia

investigations.name: Renal Function Tests
investigations.indication: Assess renal handling and safety of potassium replacement

counselling: Low potassium can affect muscle strength and heart rhythm. — पोटैशियम कम होने से मांसपेशियों में कमज़ोरी और दिल की धड़कन बिगड़ सकती है।
Severe low potassium may need IV correction and heart monitoring. — गंभीर कमी में नस द्वारा दवा और दिल की निगरानी ज़रूरी है।
Do not stop treatment once symptoms improve; repeat blood tests are important. — लक्षण ठीक होने पर भी इलाज बंद न करें; दोबारा ब्लड टेस्ट करवाएं।
Vomiting, diarrhea, poor intake, and some medicines can worsen potassium loss. — उल्टी, दस्त, कम खाना और कुछ दवाइयों से पोटैशियम और कम हो सकता है।
Return urgently if weakness, palpitations, breathing difficulty, or persistent vomiting develops. — कमज़ोरी, धड़कन बढ़ना, साँस में तकलीफ़ या लगातार उल्टी हो तो तुरंत आएं।
warning_signs: Muscle weakness
Respiratory muscle weakness
Paralytic ileus or abdominal distension
Palpitations or irregular heartbeat
Severe vomiting
Drowsiness or worsening weakness
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/nephrologist if serum K+ <2.5 mEq/L, ECG changes, arrhythmia, respiratory weakness, refractory hypokalemia, or suspected renal/tubular disorder.
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if serum K+ <2.5 mEq/L, ECG changes, symptomatic hypokalemia, inability to tolerate oral replacement, ongoing losses, or need for IV correction.
notes: Mild hypokalemia is often asymptomatic. Severe hypokalemia can present with muscle weakness, paralytic ileus, respiratory weakness, hypotonia, areflexia, ECG changes, and arrhythmia. Correct slowly and monitor closely.
expected_course: Clinical improvement usually occurs as serum potassium rises; ECG changes and muscle symptoms improve after adequate correction, but repeated monitoring is required until stable.
key_clinical_points: Severe hypokalemia or ECG changes require urgent IV correction.
Continuous ECG monitoring is needed during rapid IV potassium replacement.
Always consider magnesium deficiency in refractory hypokalemia.

severity_assessment.mild: Serum K+ 2.5-3.5 mEq/L, usually asymptomatic or nonspecific symptoms only.
severity_assessment.moderate: Serum K+ around 2.5-3.0 mEq/L with weakness, nausea, vomiting, or ECG changes beginning to appear.
severity_assessment.severe: Serum K+ <2.5 mEq/L or any potassium level with significant ECG changes, arrhythmia, respiratory weakness, or paralysis.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: 4-6 hourly during IV correction, then daily once stable
Repeat as indicated in refractory cases

counselling: Screening is essential even if baby appears normal — बच्चा सामान्य दिखे तब भी आंखों की जांच ज़रूरी है।
Multiple follow-up visits are required — कई बार जांच करानी पड़ेगी।
Procedure is safe — जांच सुरक्षित है।
Early treatment can prevent blindness — जल्दी इलाज से अंधापन रोका जा सकता है।
Do not miss any scheduled eye examination — कोई भी आंख की जांच की तारीख़ न छोड़ें।
Premature babies need long-term eye follow-up — समय से पहले जन्मे बच्चों को लंबे समय तक आंखों की जांच चाहिए।
warning_signs: White reflex in pupil — आंख की पुतली में सफ़ेद चमक
Abnormal eye movements — आंखों की असामान्य हरकत
Baby not following objects with eyes — बच्चा आंखों से चीज़ों को न देखना
Squinting — तिरछा देखना
Missed screening appointment — जांच की तारीख़ छूटना
expected_course: ROP screening begins at 2-4 weeks postnatal age depending on gestational age. Most preterm infants will have immature retina that vascularizes normally. Type 2 ROP requires close monitoring. Type 1 ROP requires urgent treatment (laser therapy or anti-VEGF). Screening continues until full retinal vascularization or PMA >=45 weeks without active disease.
key_clinical_points: Screen all infants with GA <=34 weeks or birth weight <=2000 grams.
First screening by 30 days of life (earlier if very premature or sick).
Pupil dilation with Tropicamide 0.5% + Phenylephrine 2.5% before examination.
Document zone, stage, and presence of plus disease at each screening.
Type 1 ROP requires treatment within 48-72 hours of diagnosis.
Stop screening when full vascularization into Zone III or PMA >=45 weeks without active disease.

severity_assessment.mild: No ROP or Stage 1 without plus disease — routine follow-up every 1-2 weeks.
severity_assessment.moderate: Stage 2 or any stage with plus disease features — weekly monitoring, close ophthalmology follow-up.
severity_assessment.severe: Stage 3 or more, Type 1 ROP, aggressive posterior ROP — immediate referral for laser therapy or anti-VEGF treatment.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Every 1-2 weeks until retina fully vascularized

diagnosis_name: Common Cold (Acute Viral Upper Respiratory Infection)

first_line_drugs.notes: 10–15 mg/kg/dose PO every 6–8 hours SOS for fever >100°F, maximum 60 mg/kg/day, duration 3–5 days
2–3 drops in each nostril 4–6 times/day for nasal blockage relief and feeding improvement

second_line_drugs.notes: Oral drops as per weight (typically 2–6 drops TDS depending on age), use only if significant nasal discharge/congestion and child is symptomatic; avoid use >5 days and use cautiously in infants

investigations.name: CBC
investigations.indication: Persistent fever >3 days, toxic appearance, suspicion of bacterial infection

investigations.name: CRP
investigations.indication: To rule out bacterial infection if symptoms persist or worsen

counselling: This is a common viral illness and usually resolves on its own within few days — यह एक सामान्य वायरल बीमारी है, कुछ दिनों में अपने आप ठीक हो जाती है।
Continue breastfeeding and give adequate fluids; small frequent feeds are better — माँ का दूध जारी रखें और पर्याप्त तरल पदार्थ दें; थोड़ा-थोड़ा बार-बार पिलाएं।
Use medicines only as prescribed; avoid over-the-counter cough syrups in infants — केवल डॉक्टर की बताई दवा दें; छोटे बच्चों को बाज़ार की खांसी की दवा न दें।
Keep child warm, avoid cold exposure, and maintain hygiene — बच्चे को गर्म रखें, ठंड से बचाएं और साफ़-सफ़ाई रखें।
Follow-up after 2–3 days or earlier if symptoms worsen — 2-3 दिन बाद या लक्षण बिगड़ें तो पहले डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।
warning_signs: Fast breathing or chest indrawing
Bluish lips or tongue
Convulsions or fits
Excessive sleepiness or unresponsiveness
Persistent vomiting
Poor feeding or refusal to feed
Fever lasting more than 3 days
Drop in oxygen saturation
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatrician or higher center if no improvement after 3 days, recurrent episodes, or development of warning signs
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if SpO2 <92%, severe respiratory distress, poor feeding, dehydration, lethargy, or age <2 months with symptoms
notes: Viral upper respiratory infection; self-limiting illness lasting 5–7 days; antibiotics are not indicated routinely
expected_course: Fever may last 2–3 days; nasal symptoms 5–7 days; cough may persist up to 7–10 days
key_clinical_points: Most common cause is viral; no role of antibiotics
Nasal saline is safest and most effective symptomatic relief in infants
Avoid strong cough syrups in children below 5 years
Maintain hydration and feeding is key to recovery

severity_assessment.mild: Runny nose, mild cough, no respiratory distress, feeding well
severity_assessment.moderate: Fever, irritability, reduced feeding, mild nasal blockage
severity_assessment.severe: Respiratory distress, hypoxia, poor feeding, lethargy

investigations.name: Color Doppler
investigations.indication: Assess testicular perfusion in suspected incarceration

counselling: Hernias do not resolve spontaneously and require surgery — हर्निया अपने आप ठीक नहीं होता, ऑपरेशन ज़रूरी है
Hydroceles often resolve without treatment — हाइड्रोसील अक्सर बिना इलाज के ठीक हो जाता है
Seek urgent care if swelling becomes painful or irreducible — अगर सूजन दर्दनाक हो या अंदर न जाए तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
Surgery is safe with excellent outcomes — ऑपरेशन सुरक्षित है और परिणाम बहुत अच्छे होते हैं
Monitor swelling size and behavior — सूजन के आकार और बदलाव पर नज़र रखें
warning_signs: Irreducible swelling (does not go back in) — सूजन जो अंदर न जाए
Sudden increase in size of swelling — सूजन का अचानक बढ़ जाना
Tender, tense or red swelling — दर्दनाक, तनी हुई या लाल सूजन
Vomiting or abdominal distension — उल्टी या पेट फूलना
Refusal to feed or excessive crying — दूध न पीना या बहुत रोना
Fever — बुखार
expected_course: Inguinal hernia: requires surgical repair (herniotomy) within 1-2 weeks of diagnosis. In preterm infants, surgery may be done before discharge. Non-communicating hydrocele: usually resolves by 12-18 months. Communicating hydrocele: may need surgery if persistent beyond 12 months or increasing in size.
key_clinical_points: Hernia: varies with crying, reducible, transillumination negative
Hydrocele: constant size, not reducible, transillumination positive
Incarcerated hernia is a surgical emergency - irreducible, tender, vomiting
Risk of incarceration is highest in first year - early surgical referral for hernia
Preterm infants have higher risk of inguinal hernia
Bilateral inguinal hernia is more common in preterm babies
Always examine contralateral side - bilateral hernias occur in 10-15% of neonates

severity_assessment.mild: Small reducible hernia or non-communicating hydrocele - elective surgical referral for hernia, observation for hydrocele
severity_assessment.moderate: Large hernia, communicating hydrocele increasing in size - early surgical referral, close monitoring
severity_assessment.severe: Incarcerated or strangulated hernia - emergency surgical management, immediate hospital referral

monitoring_parameters.frequency: At each visit; parents to monitor daily
At initial assessment and if character changes
Daily by parents; at each visit
At each visit to rule out testicular torsion

diagnosis_name: Acute Kidney Injury (AKI) with Anuric / Oligoanuric Regimen

first_line_drugs.notes: Use as maintenance fluid for insensible water loss in anuric AKI. Daily fluid = 400 mL/m²/day or about 40% of total daily fluid requirement in infants.
Replace previous 24-hour urine output mL for mL in addition to insensible loss calculation.
Add to replacement fluid only if hypokalemia is present and serum potassium is monitored.

second_line_drugs.notes: May be considered only in selected fluid-overloaded patients with residual renal responsiveness; not routine in established anuric AKI.
Indicated if fluid restriction fails or if refractory hyperkalemia, severe acidosis, uremic symptoms, or fluid overload develops.

investigations.name: Serum Electrolytes (Na, K, Cl, HCO3)
investigations.indication: Assess hyperkalemia, hyponatremia, acidosis, and fluid prescription safety

investigations.name: Urine Output Charting
investigations.indication: Needed for fluid replacement calculation and AKI severity monitoring

investigations.name: Body Weight
investigations.indication: Daily fluid balance and fluid overload assessment

investigations.name: ABG / VBG
investigations.indication: If metabolic acidosis or respiratory compromise suspected

investigations.name: Ultrasound KUB
investigations.indication: Evaluate cause of AKI and exclude obstruction

counselling: In anuric AKI, fluid intake must be carefully restricted to avoid overload. — पेशाब बंद होने पर पानी और तरल पदार्थ सीमित रखना बहुत जरूरी है।
Daily fluid is calculated from insensible loss plus measured urine output. — रोज़ाना का पानी शरीर की ज़रूरत और पेशाब की मात्रा से तय होता है।
Do not add potassium unless blood potassium is low and the doctor advises it. — जब तक डॉक्टर न कहें, पोटैशियम न दें।
Strict intake-output charting and daily weight are essential. — पानी का हिसाब और रोज़ वजन लेना बहुत जरूरी है।
Dialysis may be needed if kidney function does not improve or complications appear. — अगर किडनी ठीक नहीं होती तो डायलिसिस की जरूरत पड़ सकती है।
warning_signs: No urine or very low urine output
Breathing difficulty or fast breathing
Swelling of face or body
Drowsiness or irritability
Palpitations or weakness due to hyperkalemia
Seizures or worsening edema
referral_criteria: Refer urgently to pediatric nephrologist / intensivist if persistent anuria, rising creatinine, refractory electrolyte imbalance, severe acidosis, hypertension, or fluid overload.
hospitalisation_criteria: All children with anuric AKI need admission for strict monitoring, IV fluid regulation, and serial biochemical assessment.
notes: Daily fluid requirement in anuric AKI = insensible water loss + previous 24-hour urine output. Insensible water loss may be estimated as 400 mL/m²/day. BSA formula: ([4 x weight in kg] + 7) / (90 + weight in kg). Add KCl only if hypokalemia is present.
source: Provided pediatric anuric regimen in AKI protocol
expected_course: Fluid prescription is revised every 24 hours based on urine output, weight, and labs. Improvement depends on underlying cause of AKI; some children may progress to dialysis if complications develop.
key_clinical_points: In anuric AKI, fluid overload prevention is the main goal.
Use insensible loss plus urine output, not full maintenance, for daily fluid prescription.
Potassium should not be routinely added unless hypokalemia is documented.

severity_assessment.mild: Reduced urine output with stable electrolytes and no fluid overload.
severity_assessment.moderate: Oliguria/anuria with rising creatinine or mild electrolyte / fluid imbalance.
severity_assessment.severe: Persistent anuria, fluid overload, severe acidosis, hyperkalemia, hypertension, or uremic symptoms.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: 12-24 hourly or more often if unstable

counselling: Most birth injuries are mild and heal without long-term problems — ज़्यादातर जन्म-चोटें हल्की होती हैं और बिना किसी लंबी समस्या के ठीक हो जाती हैं
Minor scalp swelling or bruising often settles on its own — सिर की हल्की सूजन या नील अपने आप ठीक हो जाती है
Handle the baby gently — बच्चे को नरमी से पकड़ें
Do not pull or lift forcefully under the arms — बच्चे को बाज़ुओं के नीचे से ज़ोर से न उठाएं
Follow-up is important if there is decreased limb movement or persistent swelling — अगर हाथ-पैर की हलचल कम हो या सूजन बनी रहे तो जांच ज़रूरी है
Seek urgent care if baby becomes pale, lethargic, poorly feeding, or swelling increases — अगर बच्चा पीला, सुस्त, कम दूध पीने वाला हो जाए या सूजन बढ़ जाए तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Expanding or tense swelling on scalp — सिर पर बढ़ती या तनी हुई सूजन
Baby not moving one arm or leg — बच्चे का एक हाथ या पैर न हिलाना
Facial asymmetry during crying — रोते समय चेहरे का एक तरफ़ लटकना
Pale or blue color — बच्चे का पीला या नीला पड़ जाना
Seizures or excessive irritability — दौरे या बहुत ज़्यादा चिड़चिड़ापन
Poor feeding or vomiting — ठीक से दूध न पीना या उल्टी
Increasing jaundice — बढ़ता हुआ पीलिया
referral_criteria: Suspected subgaleal hemorrhage
Shock or poor perfusion
Persistent seizures
Organ injury suspected
Depressed skull fracture
Persistent brachial plexus weakness
Major fracture requiring splinting/orthopedic care
expected_course: Caput succedaneum resolves within 48-72 hours. Cephalhematoma may take 2-8 weeks to resolve and may calcify. Clavicle fractures heal within 2-3 weeks with callus formation. Erb palsy recovers in 80-90% of cases within 3-6 months. Facial nerve palsy usually resolves within 2-3 weeks. Subgaleal hemorrhage is a medical emergency requiring immediate NICU care.
key_clinical_points: Caput succedaneum crosses suture lines; cephalhematoma does not
Cephalhematoma may cause significant jaundice requiring phototherapy
Clavicle fracture: Moro reflex asymmetry is the key clinical sign
Erb palsy: waiter tip posture (arm adducted, internally rotated, wrist flexed)
Subgaleal hemorrhage can cause hypovolemic shock - monitor head circumference serially
Always check for associated injuries when one birth injury is found
Document mode of delivery and risk factors carefully

severity_assessment.mild: Caput succedaneum, small bruises, small cephalhematoma - observation and reassurance
severity_assessment.moderate: Clavicle fracture, Erb palsy, facial nerve palsy, large cephalhematoma - active monitoring, immobilization, physiotherapy referral
severity_assessment.severe: Subgaleal hemorrhage, skull fracture, organ injury, persistent neurological deficit - emergency stabilization, NICU admission, surgical referral

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Daily for first 3 days if cephalhematoma or subgaleal hemorrhage
If cephalhematoma - check at 48-72 hours and as clinically indicated
At each assessment for brachial plexus or fracture cases
Continuously if subgaleal hemorrhage; otherwise at each visit

localized_no_systemic_signs: CBC and CRP if not improving

counselling: Keep umbilical cord clean and dry. — नाल को साफ़ और सूखा रखें।
Avoid tight clothing over cord. — नाल पर कसे कपड़े न पहनाएं।
Do not apply oils, powders or traditional substances. — तेल, पाउडर या घरेलू चीज़ें न लगाएं।
Watch for warning signs: redness, swelling, foul smell, pus, fever. — लालिमा, सूजन, बदबू, पीप या बुखार पर ध्यान दें।
Normal cord separation occurs in 7–14 days. — नाल आमतौर पर 7-14 दिन में गिर जाती है।
Seek medical help if discharge or redness increases. — रिसाव या लालिमा बढ़े तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।
warning_signs: Purulent or foul-smelling umbilical discharge — नाल से पीप या बदबू आना
Spreading redness beyond 1 cm from stump — नाल से 1 सेमी से ज़्यादा लालिमा फैलना
Fever or hypothermia — बुखार या शरीर ठंडा पड़ना
Lethargy or poor feeding — सुस्ती या दूध न पीना
Abdominal distension — पेट का फूलना
Skin discoloration around umbilicus — नाभि के आस-पास त्वचा का रंग बदलना
expected_course: Localized mild omphalitis with topical treatment usually resolves within 5-7 days. Moderate to severe omphalitis requires hospitalization with IV antibiotics (Ampicillin + Gentamicin) for 7-10 days. Complications like necrotizing fasciitis, peritonitis, or systemic sepsis are life-threatening and require urgent surgical intervention. Mortality is 7-15% even with treatment in severe cases.
key_clinical_points: Omphalitis is a neonatal emergency that can progress rapidly to sepsis
Redness spreading >0.5-1 cm from stump base is concerning
Most common organisms: Staph aureus, Group A Streptococcus, Gram-negative bacilli
Home delivery with unsterile cord care is the biggest risk factor in India
4% chlorhexidine application at birth reduces omphalitis risk (WHO recommendation)
Delayed cord separation (>3 weeks) with discharge may indicate underlying immune deficiency

severity_assessment.mild: Minimal periumbilical erythema (<0.5 cm), no systemic signs, baby feeding well — topical mupirocin, dry cord care, close follow-up
severity_assessment.moderate: Erythema 0.5-2 cm, mild induration, no systemic signs — oral antibiotics, daily review, consider admission
severity_assessment.severe: Spreading cellulitis >2 cm, systemic signs (fever, lethargy, poor feeding), pus — immediate admission, IV Ampicillin + Gentamicin, surgical consult

diagnosis_name: Blood in Stool in a Neonate
counselling: Small streaks of blood are often benign (anal fissure or allergy) — खून की हल्की लकीरें अक्सर सामान्य होती हैं (गुदा में दरार या एलर्जी)
Reassure if baby is active and feeding well — अगर बच्चा सक्रिय है और अच्छे से दूध पी रहा है तो चिंता न करें
Avoid frequent rectal temperature checks — बार-बार गुदा से तापमान न लें
Continue breastfeeding unless advised otherwise — जब तक डॉक्टर न कहे, माँ का दूध पिलाना जारी रखें
Watch for warning signs: fever, distension, vomiting, poor feeding — खतरे के संकेत देखें: बुखार, पेट फूलना, उल्टी, कम दूध पीना
Seek urgent care if symptoms worsen — अगर लक्षण बिगड़ें तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Large amount of blood in stool — पखाने में ज़्यादा खून आना
Abdominal distension or hardness — पेट का फूलना या सख्त होना
Bilious (green) vomiting — हरे रंग की उल्टी
Fever or hypothermia — बुखार या शरीर ठंडा पड़ना
Baby becoming lethargic or pale — बच्चे का सुस्त या पीला पड़ जाना
Refusal to feed — दूध न पीना
Black tarry stools — काला चिपचिपा पखाना
referral_criteria: Suspected NEC
Sepsis or shock
Persistent or large bleeding
Abdominal distension or obstruction
Failure to thrive
Coagulopathy suspected
expected_course: Anal fissure: resolves within a few days with gentle care. CMPA-related blood in stool: improves within 2-4 weeks of maternal dairy elimination. Swallowed maternal blood: self-limiting, resolves in 1-2 days. NEC or sepsis: requires hospitalization and may take weeks to recover.
key_clinical_points: APT test differentiates swallowed maternal blood from neonatal GI bleeding
Anal fissure is the most common cause in well-appearing neonates
CMPA should be suspected if blood with mucus in breastfed baby
NEC is more common in preterm infants — triad of abdominal distension, bloody stools, pneumatosis intestinalis
Always check coagulation profile if bleeding disorder suspected
Vitamin K administration status must be verified
Hemodynamic stability assessment is the first priority

severity_assessment.mild: Streaks of blood, well baby, feeding normally, no distension — observation, maternal dairy trial if CMPA suspected
severity_assessment.moderate: Mucoid bloody stools, feeding adequately but with mild discomfort — investigations (stool culture, fecal calprotectin), close follow-up
severity_assessment.severe: Large blood volume, distension, vomiting, lethargy, shock signs — NPO, IV fluids, antibiotics, NICU admission, surgical consult

monitoring_parameters.frequency: If significant bleeding — at presentation and repeat in 12-24 hours
Daily if admitted, at follow-up if outpatient

counselling: Not all jaundice is benign after 2 weeks of age. — 2 हफ्ते बाद भी पीलिया सामान्य नहीं माना जाना चाहिए।
Pale stools and dark urine are danger signs and need urgent evaluation. — सफ़ेद पोटी और गहरे रंग का पेशाब खतरे के निशान हैं, तुरंत जांच कराएं।
Early surgery in biliary atresia can preserve native liver function. — पित्त नली की बीमारी में जल्दी ऑपरेशन से लीवर बचाया जा सकता है।
Good nutrition and vitamin supplementation are important. — अच्छा पोषण और विटामिन की खुराक ज़रूरी है।
Regular follow-up and monitoring are essential. — नियमित जांच बहुत ज़रूरी है।
warning_signs: Pale or white stools — सफ़ेद या मिट्टी जैसी पोटी
Dark yellow or brown urine — गहरे पीले या भूरे रंग का पेशाब
Worsening jaundice — पीलिया बढ़ना
Bleeding from any site — कहीं से भी खून आना
Poor feeding or weight loss — दूध न पीना या वज़न कम होना
Swollen abdomen — पेट फूलना
expected_course: Neonatal cholestasis requires urgent evaluation. Biliary atresia must be ruled out within the first 45-60 days of life for best surgical outcomes (Kasai procedure). Metabolic and infectious causes may respond to specific treatment. Without timely intervention, progressive liver damage and cirrhosis can occur.
key_clinical_points: Any jaundice persisting beyond 14 days in a term infant must be fractionated (total + direct bilirubin).
Direct bilirubin >1 mg/dL (if total <5) or >20% of total (if total >5) defines cholestasis.
Stool color card is a simple, effective screening tool for parents.
High GGT (>300 IU/L) with hepatomegaly is suggestive of biliary atresia.
Kasai portoenterostomy should ideally be performed before 45-60 days of age.
Fat-soluble vitamin supplementation (A, D, E, K) is essential in cholestasis.

severity_assessment.mild: Persistent jaundice with normal direct bilirubin, normal stool color, thriving baby — monitor and recheck.
severity_assessment.moderate: Elevated direct bilirubin, slightly abnormal LFTs, normal stool color — complete workup urgently.
severity_assessment.severe: Pale/acholic stools, high GGT, hepatomegaly, coagulopathy, failure to thrive — urgent specialist referral, suspect biliary atresia.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Weekly until resolved or diagnosis confirmed
Daily by parents using stool color card

counselling: Some jaundice after 2 weeks can be common, especially in breastfed babies. — 2 हफ्ते बाद भी पीलिया हो सकता है, खासकर माँ का दूध पीने वाले बच्चों में।
Breastfeeding is usually safe and should generally be continued. — माँ का दूध सुरक्षित है और आमतौर पर जारी रखना चाहिए।
Further tests are needed mainly to rule out uncommon but important causes. — जांच कुछ दुर्लभ लेकिन गंभीर कारणों को दूर करने के लिए ज़रूरी है।
Parents should monitor feeding, urine color, stool color, activity and weight gain. — दूध पीना, पेशाब का रंग, पोटी का रंग, हिलना-डुलना और वज़न बढ़ना ध्यान से देखें।
Follow-up is important until jaundice resolves. — पीलिया ठीक होने तक नियमित जांच कराएं।
warning_signs: Dark urine — गहरे रंग का पेशाब
Pale or white stools — सफ़ेद या हल्के रंग की पोटी
Poor feeding or lethargy — दूध न पीना या सुस्ती
Worsening jaundice — पीलिया बढ़ना
Fever or illness — बुखार या बीमारी
Bleeding from any site — कहीं से खून आना
expected_course: Breast milk jaundice (the most common cause) is benign and self-limiting, typically resolving by 3-4 months of age. Breastfeeding should be continued. However, treatable conditions like congenital hypothyroidism, hemolysis, and cholestasis must be ruled out before labeling jaundice as breast milk jaundice.
key_clinical_points: Always fractionate bilirubin (total + direct) before attributing jaundice to breast milk.
Prolonged jaundice cutoff: >14 days (term), >21 days (preterm).
TSH and Free T4 must be checked to rule out congenital hypothyroidism.
Reticulocyte count, blood group, Coombs test, and G6PD screen for hemolysis.
Normal stool and urine color with a thriving baby suggests breast milk jaundice.
Never label jaundice as benign until conjugated bilirubin is confirmed normal.

severity_assessment.mild: Thriving baby with unconjugated jaundice, normal stools, normal feeding — likely breast milk jaundice, monitor.
severity_assessment.moderate: Unconjugated jaundice with borderline weight gain or mildly elevated reticulocytes — complete hemolysis and thyroid workup.
severity_assessment.severe: Elevated direct bilirubin, pale stools, dark urine, sick baby, hepatosplenomegaly — urgent cholestasis/liver workup and referral.

counselling: Mild jaundice is common but timing and progression are important — हल्का पीलापन आम है लेकिन समय और बढ़ोतरी पर ध्यान दें
Ensure adequate feeding (8–12 feeds/day) — दिन में 8-12 बार दूध पिलाएं
Return if jaundice worsens or feeding decreases — पीलापन बढ़े या दूध कम पिए तो तुरंत आएं
Educate about danger signs — खतरे के लक्षण पहचानें: सुस्ती, तेज़ रोना, पीठ तनना, बुखार
warning_signs: Jaundice within first 24 hours of life — जन्म के 24 घंटे में पीलापन (हमेशा खतरनाक)
Poor feeding or lethargy — दूध न पीना या सुस्ती
High-pitched cry — तेज़ पतली आवाज़ में रोना
Back arching (opisthotonus) — पीठ का तनना
Fever or pallor — बुखार या पीलापन
Rapidly worsening jaundice — तेज़ी से बढ़ता पीलापन
Dark urine or pale stools — गहरे रंग का पेशाब या सफ़ेद मल
expected_course: Physiological jaundice typically appears after 24 hours, peaks at day 3-5 in term neonates and day 5-7 in preterms, and resolves by 2 weeks. Breastfeeding jaundice may persist longer but is benign if baby is thriving. Pathological jaundice (onset <24h, rapid rise, hemolysis) requires urgent investigation and phototherapy. Kernicterus is preventable with timely intervention.
key_clinical_points: Jaundice <24 hours is ALWAYS pathological — investigate immediately
Bhutani nomogram guides phototherapy decisions based on age in hours and TSB
Ensure adequate breastfeeding (8-12 feeds/day) to promote bilirubin excretion
Check blood group and Coombs test if hemolysis suspected
G6PD deficiency is common in India and can cause severe hemolytic jaundice
TSB >20 mg/dL in term neonate is dangerous and requires urgent phototherapy

severity_assessment.mild: Jaundice limited to face and chest, good feeding, TSB in low-risk zone on nomogram — reassurance, close follow-up in 24-48h
severity_assessment.moderate: Jaundice extending to abdomen, TSB in intermediate zone, risk factors present — repeat TSB, close monitoring, consider phototherapy
severity_assessment.severe: Jaundice to palms/soles, TSB in high-risk zone, signs of encephalopathy — urgent admission, intensive phototherapy, consider exchange transfusion

counselling: Neonatal fever is serious and requires admission — नवजात शिशु में बुखार गंभीर है और भर्ती होना ज़रूरी है।
Early treatment improves outcome — जल्दी इलाज शुरू करने से बच्चा जल्दी ठीक होता है।
Avoid overheating or over-wrapping — बच्चे को ज़्यादा कपड़े या गर्मी में न रखें।
Watch for danger signs — खतरे के संकेतों पर ध्यान रखें।
warning_signs: Poor feeding or refusal to feed
Lethargy or excessive sleepiness
Seizures or abnormal movements
Respiratory distress or fast breathing
Cyanosis (bluish discoloration)
Bulging fontanelle
Vesicular skin rash (suspect HSV)
Jaundice worsening rapidly
Hypothermia (temperature <36.5 C)
expected_course: Depends on underlying cause. Bacterial infections require 7-14 days of antibiotics. Viral infections may resolve in 3-5 days. HSV requires 14-21 days of acyclovir. Early sepsis workup and empirical antibiotics are critical as delays worsen prognosis significantly.
key_clinical_points: All febrile neonates (<28 days) must be admitted for full sepsis workup.
Send all cultures BEFORE starting antibiotics.
HSV must be considered in any febrile neonate, especially with vesicular rash, seizures, or liver involvement.
Even a well-appearing febrile neonate has significant risk of serious bacterial infection.
Hypothermia in a neonate is as concerning as fever.

severity_assessment.mild: Low-grade fever with well-appearing neonate, normal feeding, and no risk factors -- still requires full workup and admission.
severity_assessment.moderate: Fever with reduced feeding, mild irritability, or maternal risk factors (PROM, GBS).
severity_assessment.severe: High fever with lethargy, seizures, respiratory distress, poor perfusion, bulging fontanelle, or hemodynamic instability.

counselling: Most newborn rashes are harmless and self-resolving — ज़्यादातर नवजात के दाने हानिरहित होते हैं और अपने आप ठीक हो जाते हैं
No need for creams, antibiotics, or medications — किसी क्रीम, एंटीबायोटिक या दवा की ज़रूरत नहीं है
Avoid talcum powder and scented products — टैल्कम पाउडर और खुशबूदार चीज़ों से बचें
Maintain hygiene with plain lukewarm water bathing — गुनगुने पानी से साफ़ सफ़ाई रखें
Rash will gradually resolve over days to weeks — दाने धीरे-धीरे कुछ दिनों से हफ़्तों में ठीक हो जाएंगे
Seek medical care if red flag symptoms appear — अगर खतरे के संकेत दिखें तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Fever with rash — दाने के साथ बुखार
Blisters or pus-filled bumps — छाले या मवाद वाले दाने
Rash spreading rapidly — दाने तेज़ी से फैलना
Baby not feeding well — बच्चे का ठीक से दूध न पीना
Baby becoming lethargic or irritable — बच्चे का सुस्त या चिड़चिड़ा हो जाना
Purple or dark red spots on skin — त्वचा पर बैंगनी या गहरे लाल धब्बे
expected_course: Most benign neonatal rashes appear within the first 1–2 weeks of life and resolve spontaneously within days to weeks without any treatment. Erythema toxicum typically appears on day 1–3 and resolves by 5–14 days. Milia resolve within 4 weeks. Neonatal acne may take 1–3 months to clear.
key_clinical_points: Erythema toxicum is the most common benign neonatal rash — seen in up to 50% of term neonates
Milia are tiny white papules on the nose and face — do not squeeze
Neonatal acne is hormonal and self-limiting
Transient neonatal pustular melanosis is more common in dark-skinned neonates
Cutis marmorata is a normal vasomotor response to cold
Key differentiator: benign rashes occur in well, afebrile neonates with good feeding
Investigate only if systemic signs (fever, poor feeding, hepatosplenomegaly) are present
Avoid unnecessary topical steroids or antibiotics

severity_assessment.mild: Localized rash, well baby, feeding normally, afebrile — observation and reassurance only
severity_assessment.moderate: Widespread rash but baby otherwise well — close observation, consider dermatology review if persistent beyond 2 weeks
severity_assessment.severe: Rash with fever, vesicles/bullae, poor feeding, lethargy, or systemic signs — investigate for infection (CBC, CRP, blood culture, skin swab), consider NICU admission

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Daily by parents, at each OPD visit
If any new rash or systemic concern

diagnosis_name: Breast Masses, Vaginal Bleeding and Other Genital Anomalies in Neonates
counselling: These findings are common and usually hormonal / physiological — ये लक्षण आम हैं और आमतौर पर हार्मोन के कारण होते हैं
Breast swelling or milky discharge often resolves spontaneously — स्तन की सूजन या दूध जैसा रिसाव अपने आप ठीक हो जाता है
Do not squeeze or massage the breasts — स्तनों को दबाएं या मालिश न करें
First-week vaginal bleeding is usually self-limiting — पहले हफ्ते में योनि से खून आना आमतौर पर अपने आप रुक जाता है
Maintain gentle hygiene only; no internal cleaning — सिर्फ़ हल्की सफ़ाई रखें; अंदर की सफ़ाई न करें

Seek review if bleeding persists, discharge is foul-smelling, breast becomes red/tender, or genitalia appear unclear — अगर खून बंद न हो, बदबूदार रिसाव हो, स्तन लाल/दर्दनाक हो, या जननांग स्पष्ट न दिखें तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Persistent vaginal bleeding beyond first week — पहले हफ्ते के बाद भी योनि से खून आना
Breast redness, warmth or pus — स्तन पर लालिमा, गर्मी या मवाद
Ambiguous genitalia — अस्पष्ट जननांग
Fever or poor feeding — बुखार या कम दूध पीना
Inguinal swelling that does not reduce — जांघ की सूजन जो कम न हो
Signs of salt loss: vomiting, poor weight gain, dehydration — नमक की कमी के संकेत: उल्टी, वज़न न बढ़ना, पानी की कमी
referral_criteria: Ambiguous genitalia
Persistent vaginal bleeding
Breast abscess / mastitis
Hard or non-reducible inguinal / labial mass
Suspected imperforate hymen / cloacal anomaly
Salt-loss symptoms or suspicion of CAH
expected_course: Neonatal breast enlargement resolves within 2-4 weeks as maternal hormones clear. Witch milk (galactorrhea) may persist for up to 2 months. First-week vaginal bleeding (estrogen withdrawal) resolves within 3-5 days. Mucoid vaginal discharge may persist for 2-4 weeks. All are self-limiting and benign.
key_clinical_points: Neonatal breast enlargement is due to transplacental maternal estrogen — never squeeze
Squeezing breasts can cause mastitis/abscess — educate caregivers strongly
Vaginal bleeding in first week is estrogen withdrawal — benign if small amount
Always examine for ambiguous genitalia — rule out CAH (check 17-OHP, electrolytes)
Imperforate hymen presents as bulging introitus — needs surgical referral
Breast abscess requires antibiotics and possibly drainage
Salt-losing CAH is a neonatal emergency — vomiting, dehydration, hyperkalemia

severity_assessment.mild: Bilateral breast swelling, clear/milky nipple discharge, small amount vaginal bleeding in first week — reassurance only
severity_assessment.moderate: Unilateral tender breast, persistent bleeding, prominent clitoris — investigation (CBC, 17-OHP, electrolytes), close follow-up
severity_assessment.severe: Breast abscess, ambiguous genitalia, salt loss symptoms, hard inguinal mass — urgent evaluation, specialist referral, possible NICU admission

counselling: Spitting up is common and improves by 4–6 months
Avoid overfeeding and home remedies
Maintain proper feeding position and burping
Return immediately if green vomiting, fever, poor feeding, or weight loss
Monitor baby's growth and activity
warning_signs: Bilious (green/yellow) vomiting — हरी या पीली उल्टी
Projectile vomiting — तेज़ ज़ोर से उल्टी आना
Blood in vomit — उल्टी में खून आना
Abdominal distension — पेट का फूलना
Failure to pass meconium within 24 hours — 24 घंटे में पहला मल न आना
Lethargy or poor feeding — सुस्ती या दूध न पीना
Dehydration (dry mouth, sunken eyes, no tears) — पानी की कमी
Fever or hypothermia — बुखार या शरीर का ठंडा पड़ना
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric surgeon if bilious vomiting, abdominal distension, or suspected intestinal obstruction. Refer to pediatric GI if persistent non-bilious vomiting not responding to conservative management.
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if bilious vomiting, projectile vomiting with dehydration, suspected surgical cause, sepsis, or failure to thrive.
notes: Bilious vomiting in a neonate is malrotation with volvulus until proven otherwise. Requires urgent upper GI contrast study.
source: NNF Guidelines 2024, Nelson Textbook of Pediatrics 22e
expected_course: Most functional vomiting (GER, overfeeding) resolves within days with positional and feeding modifications. Bilious or projectile vomiting requires urgent evaluation. Pyloric stenosis presents at 3-6 weeks with progressive projectile vomiting.
key_clinical_points: Bilious vomiting in a neonate is a surgical emergency until proven otherwise
Always check for abdominal distension and bowel sounds
Assess hydration status and weight change
Pyloric stenosis: non-bilious projectile vomiting at 3-6 weeks, olive-shaped mass
Sepsis can present as vomiting with lethargy in neonates
