severity_assessment.mild: Occasional spit-up after feeds, gaining weight, no distress
severity_assessment.moderate: Frequent vomiting, some weight loss, mild dehydration, irritability
severity_assessment.severe: Bilious/projectile vomiting, significant dehydration, abdominal distension, lethargy

counselling: Tongue tie is a common cause of breastfeeding difficulty in newborns — जीभ का बंधन (टंग टाई) स्तनपान में कठिनाई का आम कारण है
Mild tongue tie without feeding issues may only need observation — हल्के टंग टाई में सिर्फ़ निगरानी काफ़ी हो सकती है
Frenotomy is generally safe, quick, and effective when indicated — फ्रेनोटॉमी सुरक्षित, जल्दी और असरदार प्रक्रिया है
Most babies feed better soon after release procedure — अधिकतर बच्चे प्रक्रिया के बाद बेहतर दूध पीते हैं
Simple anterior tongue tie usually does not require general anesthesia — साधारण टंग टाई में बेहोशी की ज़रूरत नहीं होती
Seek review if feeding remains poor or bleeding occurs after procedure — अगर दूध पीने में सुधार न हो या खून आए तो दोबारा दिखाएं
warning_signs: Persistent poor latch despite correct positioning — सही स्थिति में भी दूध सही से न पकड़ पाना
Inadequate weight gain — वज़न पर्याप्त न बढ़ना
Maternal nipple trauma or bleeding — माँ के निप्पल में दर्द या खून
Heart-shaped or notched tongue tip — जीभ का दिल जैसा आकार
Clicking sounds during feeds — दूध पीते समय क्लिक की आवाज़
expected_course: Mild anterior tongue tie without feeding difficulty may not require intervention. If frenotomy is performed, immediate improvement in latch is often seen. Post-procedure, babies can breastfeed right away. Posterior tongue tie (Type 3-4) may require ENT or specialist referral. Speech issues may become apparent later if untreated.
key_clinical_points: Always assess feeding function, not just appearance of frenulum
Coryllos classification helps guide management decisions
Type 1-2 (anterior) often amenable to office frenotomy
Type 3-4 (posterior/submucosal) may need specialist assessment
Maternal lactation support is essential alongside surgical intervention
Re-attachment of frenulum can occur; tongue exercises may help prevent this

severity_assessment.mild: Short frenulum visible but baby feeding well, gaining weight, no maternal nipple pain — observe and reassure
severity_assessment.moderate: Feeding difficulty with poor latch, slow feeds, maternal pain — consider frenotomy, optimize positioning
severity_assessment.severe: Failure to thrive, significant maternal nipple trauma, posterior thick tie — urgent frenotomy or specialist referral

counselling: Condition is common in newborn males — नवजात लड़कों में यह आम समस्या है
Most cases resolve by 6 months — अधिकतर 6 महीने तक अपने आप ठीक हो जाती है
Surgery is simple and safe if required — ज़रूरत हो तो ऑपरेशन सरल और सुरक्षित है
Early surgery improves fertility — जल्दी ऑपरेशन से प्रजनन क्षमता बेहतर रहती है
Cancer risk is low but higher than normal — कैंसर का खतरा कम है पर सामान्य से ज़्यादा
Regular follow-up till puberty is essential — यौवन तक नियमित जांच ज़रूरी है
warning_signs: Bilateral non-palpable testes — दोनों तरफ़ अंडकोष न मिलना
Empty scrotum with ambiguous genitalia — खाली अंडकोष थैली और अस्पष्ट जननांग
Associated inguinal hernia — साथ में हर्निया होना
Ascending testis (previously normal) — पहले सामान्य था पर अब ऊपर चला गया
Testis not descended by 6 months — 6 महीने तक अंडकोष नीचे न आना
expected_course: Spontaneous descent occurs in most cases by 6 months of age, especially in term infants. If testis has not descended by 6 months, it is unlikely to descend spontaneously. Orchidopexy is recommended between 6-18 months for best fertility and malignancy outcomes. Retractile testis needs annual monitoring as it may become ascending.
key_clinical_points: Examine in warm room with calm, relaxed infant for accurate assessment
Two-handed technique: one hand from iliac crest along canal, other hand palpates scrotum
Differentiate retractile (can be brought down and stays) from true UDT (springs back)
Bilateral non-palpable testes require urgent karyotype and hormonal workup to exclude DSD
No role of routine hormonal therapy (hCG) — surgery is the definitive treatment
Orchidopexy by 18 months optimizes fertility and reduces malignancy risk

severity_assessment.mild: Unilateral palpable UDT in inguinal canal, term infant <6 months — observe, follow-up at 6 months
severity_assessment.moderate: Persistent unilateral UDT at 6 months, palpable — refer for orchidopexy (6-18 months)
severity_assessment.severe: Bilateral non-palpable testes or UDT with ambiguous genitalia — urgent pediatric surgery and endocrine evaluation

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Every 3 months until surgery or descent

investigations.name: VCUG (Voiding Cystourethrogram)
investigations.indication: Bilateral dilation, ureteral dilation, abnormal bladder, or UTI

counselling: Most cases resolve without surgery — ज़्यादातर मामले बिना ऑपरेशन के ठीक हो जाते हैं
Regular follow-up and scans are essential — नियमित जांच और अल्ट्रासाउंड ज़रूरी है
Watch for signs of UTI such as fever, irritability, or poor feeding — पेशाब के संक्रमण के संकेत देखें जैसे बुखार, चिड़चिड़ापन, या कम दूध पीना
Serial imaging helps guide treatment decisions — बार-बार की जाने वाली तस्वीरें इलाज का फैसला करने में मदद करती हैं
Early detection prevents kidney damage — जल्दी पता लगने से गुर्दे की क्षति रुकती है
warning_signs: Fever or irritability suggesting UTI — बुखार या चिड़चिड़ापन जो पेशाब के संक्रमण का संकेत हो
Poor feeding or vomiting — कम दूध पीना या उल्टी
Palpable abdominal mass — पेट में गांठ महसूस होना
Poor urine stream or dribbling — पेशाब की कमज़ोर धार या टपकना
Failure to thrive — वज़न और विकास न बढ़ना
Decreased urine output — पेशाब कम आना
expected_course: Mild hydronephrosis (Grade 1-2): resolves spontaneously in 50-70% of cases by 12-18 months. Moderate hydronephrosis (Grade 3): requires close monitoring; may need surgery if worsening. Severe hydronephrosis (Grade 4): often requires surgical intervention (pyeloplasty). PUJ obstruction is the most common pathological cause. Postnatal ultrasound should be done after day 3.
key_clinical_points: First postnatal ultrasound at 3-7 days of life (not immediately - early scan may be falsely reassuring)
SFU grading system: Grade 1-4 based on pelvic and calyceal dilation
Grade 1-2: serial ultrasound monitoring; no routine antibiotic prophylaxis
Grade 3-4: antibiotic prophylaxis, VCUG, nuclear scan (DTPA/MAG3)
Bilateral hydronephrosis or abnormal bladder: rule out posterior urethral valves
Check serum creatinine at day 3-5 (reflects neonatal renal function, not maternal)
UTI prevention with antibiotic prophylaxis in high-grade cases
Refer to pediatric urologist/nephrologist for Grade 3-4 or bilateral cases

severity_assessment.mild: Grade 1-2 unilateral hydronephrosis, normal renal parenchyma - serial ultrasound at 6 weeks, 3 months, 6 months
severity_assessment.moderate: Grade 3 or bilateral Grade 2, no parenchymal thinning - antibiotic prophylaxis, VCUG, consider nuclear scan, pediatric nephrology referral
severity_assessment.severe: Grade 4, parenchymal thinning, bilateral involvement, abnormal bladder, elevated creatinine - urgent urology referral, full imaging workup, possible surgical intervention

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Day 3-7, then 4-6 weeks, then 3 months, 6 months, 12 months (adjust based on grade)
Day 3-5 for bilateral or high-grade; repeat if abnormal
If fever or UTI symptoms; consider periodic screening in high-grade cases

counselling: Condition is common and usually benign — यह आम है और आमतौर पर गंभीर नहीं है।
Does not affect brain development or intelligence — इससे दिमाग के विकास या बुद्धि पर कोई असर नहीं पड़ता।
Early repositioning gives excellent outcomes — जल्दी करवट बदलने से बहुत अच्छे नतीजे मिलते हैं।
Head shape improves gradually with growth — सिर की शक्ल बढ़ने के साथ धीरे-धीरे ठीक होती है।
Helmet therapy rarely needed and only in selected cases — हेलमेट की ज़रूरत बहुत कम होती है।
Follow advice consistently for best results — सलाह का नियमित रूप से पालन करें।
Increase tummy time during awake hours — जागते समय बच्चे को पेट के बल ज़्यादा लिटाएं।
warning_signs: Cranial ridging or hard ridge along sutures — सिर पर कड़ी लकीर या उभार
Progressive worsening despite repositioning — करवट बदलने के बावजूद बिगड़ना
Developmental delay — विकास में देरी
Abnormal head circumference growth — सिर की गोलाई असामान्य बढ़ना या न बढ़ना
Associated syndromic features — अन्य असामान्य लक्षण दिखना
expected_course: Deformational plagiocephaly improves with repositioning therapy and increased tummy time in the majority of cases. Head shape typically normalizes by 12-18 months as the infant becomes more mobile. Helmet therapy (cranial orthosis) may be considered for persistent moderate-severe cases after 5-6 months of age. Craniosynostosis must always be ruled out.
key_clinical_points: Distinguish deformational plagiocephaly from craniosynostosis — open, non-ridged sutures favor deformational cause.
Check for associated congenital muscular torticollis (present in 20% of cases).
Tummy time is the most effective conservative treatment.
Alternate head position during sleep to reduce pressure on flat side.
Helmet therapy is effective if started between 4-12 months of age when needed.
CT scan is only indicated if craniosynostosis is suspected — avoid unnecessary radiation.

severity_assessment.mild: Slight asymmetry, no ear displacement, open sutures — repositioning and tummy time advice.
severity_assessment.moderate: Obvious asymmetry with ear displacement, forehead prominence — repositioning, physiotherapy if torticollis present, close follow-up.
severity_assessment.severe: Marked asymmetry not improving with repositioning after 2-3 months, or craniosynostosis concern — consider helmet therapy referral or imaging.

investigations.name: ECG
investigations.purpose: Supportive test; may be nonspecific in neonates

counselling: Not all murmurs are dangerous; some resolve spontaneously. — सभी दिल की आवाज़ें खतरनाक नहीं होतीं; कुछ अपने आप ठीक हो जाती हैं।
If echocardiography is advised, it is to ensure the baby's heart is normal. — अगर इको टेस्ट बोला गया है तो यह बच्चे के दिल की जांच के लिए है।
Explain warning signs: cyanosis, breathing difficulty, poor feeding. — खतरे के निशान: नीलापन, सांस में तकलीफ, दूध न पीना।
Encourage regular follow-up even after a normal echocardiogram. — सामान्य रिपोर्ट आने पर भी नियमित जांच कराएं।
warning_signs: Blue discoloration of lips or tongue — होंठ या जीभ का नीला पड़ना
Fast or difficulty breathing — तेज़ या मुश्किल सांस लेना
Poor feeding or excessive sweating during feeds — दूध न पीना या दूध पीते समय बहुत पसीना आना
Lethargy or reduced activity — सुस्ती या कम हिलना-डुलना
Cold hands and feet — हाथ-पैर ठंडे पड़ना
expected_course: Innocent murmurs usually resolve within the first few months of life as transitional circulation stabilizes. Pathological murmurs require echocardiography and may need ongoing cardiology follow-up or intervention.
key_clinical_points: Pulse oximetry screening (pre- and post-ductal) is mandatory for all neonates with a murmur.
Femoral pulse assessment is critical to rule out coarctation of aorta.
A grade >=3/6, harsh, diastolic, or pansystolic murmur should be considered pathological until proven otherwise.
Echocardiography is the gold standard investigation.
Do not delay referral if critical congenital heart disease (CCHD) is suspected.

severity_assessment.mild: Soft systolic murmur (grade 1-2/6), asymptomatic, normal saturations and femoral pulses — observe and review.
severity_assessment.moderate: Murmur persisting beyond 48 hours, any associated symptoms, or saturation concern — arrange echocardiography.
severity_assessment.severe: Loud/harsh murmur, cyanosis, desaturation, absent femoral pulses, poor feeding — urgent cardiology referral and consider CCHD.

counselling: Many neonates have immature, soft airways causing noise. — कई नवजात शिशुओं की सांस की नली कोमल होती है जिससे आवाज़ आती है।
Laryngomalacia peaks at 6 months and resolves by 12-18 months. — लैरिंगोमलेशिया 6 महीने में सबसे ज़्यादा होता है और 12-18 महीने तक ठीक हो जाता है।
Most cases are self-limiting. — ज़्यादातर मामले अपने आप ठीक हो जाते हैं।
Watch for warning signs like bluish lips, chest indrawing, choking. — नीले होंठ, छाती धंसना, या दम घुटना दिखे तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं।
Feed baby in upright position to reduce noisy breathing. — बच्चे को ऊपर उठाकर (बैठी स्थिति में) दूध पिलाएं।
warning_signs: Blue discoloration of lips — होंठों का नीला पड़ना
Chest retractions or indrawing — छाती का धंसना
Apnea or breathing pauses — सांस रुकना
Choking or aspiration during feeds — दूध पीते समय दम घुटना
Poor weight gain — वज़न न बढ़ना
Stridor at rest or worsening — आराम में भी सांस की आवाज़ या बढ़ना
expected_course: Laryngomalacia (the most common cause) typically presents within the first 2 weeks of life, peaks at 4-8 months, and resolves by 12-18 months in most cases. Symptoms worsen with crying, feeding, and supine position. Severe cases (<10%) may need surgical intervention (supraglottoplasty).
key_clinical_points: Laryngomalacia is the most common cause of noisy breathing in neonates.
Identify the type of sound: stertor (nasal), stridor (laryngeal), wheeze (intrathoracic), grunt (distress).
Grunting is always a red flag indicating respiratory distress.
Flexible laryngoscopy is the gold standard for airway evaluation.
Improvement in prone position supports a diagnosis of laryngomalacia.
Rule out choanal atresia in any neonate with nasal obstruction.

severity_assessment.mild: Intermittent noise during feeds or crying only, good weight gain, normal saturations — reassure and monitor.
severity_assessment.moderate: Persistent stridor, mild feeding difficulty, borderline weight gain — ENT referral for laryngoscopy.
severity_assessment.severe: Stridor at rest with cyanosis, apnea, chest retractions, poor feeding, weight loss — admit, oxygen, urgent ENT evaluation.

monitoring_parameters.parameter: Type and severity of noisy breathing

counselling: Many newborn movements are normal or benign. — नवजात शिशु की कई हलचलें सामान्य होती हैं।
Seizure mimics do not usually cause brain damage. — ये दौरे जैसी हलचलें आमतौर पर दिमाग को नुकसान नहीं पहुँचाती।
Avoid unnecessary panic or medication without confirmation. — बिना पुष्टि के घबराएं नहीं और दवा शुरू न करें।
Observe for red flags such as apnea, cyanosis, feeding difficulty, eye deviation, or persistent rhythmic movements. — सांस रुकना, नीलापन, दूध न पीना, आंखों का टेढ़ा होना जैसे लक्षणों पर ध्यान दें।
Most mimics resolve with time. — ये अधिकतर अपने आप ठीक हो जाती हैं।
warning_signs: Apnea with bradycardia or cyanosis — सांस रुकना, धड़कन धीमी होना या नीलापन
Eye deviation or lip smacking — आंखों का टेढ़ा होना या होंठ चटकाना
Persistent tonic or clonic movements — लगातार अकड़न या झटके
Non-suppressible movements — पकड़ने पर भी न रुकने वाली हलचल
Poor feeding or lethargy — दूध न पीना या सुस्ती
Abnormal neurological examination — असामान्य तंत्रिका जांच
expected_course: Benign neonatal sleep myoclonus resolves spontaneously by 3-6 months. Jitteriness usually resolves once metabolic cause (hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia) is corrected. Hyperekplexia may require long-term medication. Video-EEG helps differentiate true seizures from mimics. Most mimics have an excellent prognosis.
key_clinical_points: Ask parents to record a video of the episode for review
Stimulus-sensitive and suppressible movements favor mimic over seizure
Normal interictal EEG and neurological exam are reassuring
Do NOT start anticonvulsants without confirmed seizure diagnosis
Benign sleep myoclonus occurs ONLY during sleep and stops on arousal
Jitteriness may indicate underlying metabolic derangement requiring correction

severity_assessment.mild: Isolated jitteriness or brief myoclonic jerks during sleep with normal exam — observation and parental reassurance
severity_assessment.moderate: Recurrent episodes with parental anxiety, uncertain diagnosis — video documentation, metabolic screen, neurology follow-up
severity_assessment.severe: Non-suppressible rhythmic movements, eye deviation, apnea, altered tone — urgent EEG and full neonatal seizure workup, consider admission

first_line_drugs.notes: Maintain SpO2 >95%, via mask or HFNC depending on severity
10–20 ml/kg bolus IV for shock, then maintenance as per requirement
5 ml/kg bolus over 10–15 min, then 0.1–1 ml/kg/hr infusion (for raised ICP)
1–5 ml/kg IV over 20 min, repeat every 4–6 hours if needed
2–4 ml/kg IV bolus if hypoglycemia suspected/confirmed
20–40 mg/kg IV loading dose, then 10–20 mg/kg/day divided doses (for seizures)

second_line_drugs.notes: 50–100 mg/kg/day IV divided BD, if suspected meningitis/encephalitis
10 mg/kg IV every 8 hours if HSV encephalitis suspected
20 mg PE/kg IV loading if seizures persist

investigations.name: CT/MRI Brain
investigations.indication: Focal deficit, unequal pupils, suspected ICP

counselling: Child’s condition is serious and needs close monitoring in ICU — बच्चे की हालत गंभीर है और ICU में करीबी निगरानी ज़रूरी है
Maintain adequate hydration but avoid overfeeding — पर्याप्त पानी दें लेकिन ज्यादा खिलाने से बचें
Give medicines strictly as prescribed — दवाइयाँ बिल्कुल डॉक्टर के बताए अनुसार दें
Avoid unnecessary stimulation, keep environment calm — बेवजह शोर न करें, माहौल शांत रखें
Regular follow-up and monitoring are essential — नियमित जांच और निगरानी बहुत जरूरी है
warning_signs: Fast breathing / chest indrawing
Bluish lips or tongue
Convulsions / fits
Excessive sleepiness / unresponsive
Persistent vomiting
Unequal pupils or abnormal movements
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/neurologist if GCS < 12, seizures, focal deficits, or no improvement
hospitalisation_criteria: GCS < 12, seizures, raised ICP signs, shock, hypoxia, inability to maintain airway
notes: Treat ABC first. Always assume raised ICP until proven otherwise. Avoid fluid overload and hyperventilation unless acute ICP crisis.
source: IAP Pediatric Emergency Guidelines, WHO 2023, AAP Pediatric Neurology
expected_course: Depends on cause. Acute stabilization in 24–72 hours; recovery varies from days to weeks.
key_clinical_points: Always prioritize airway, breathing, circulation
Treat hypoglycemia immediately
Raised ICP should be assumed in all coma cases

severity_assessment.mild: Drowsy but arousable, stable vitals
severity_assessment.moderate: Confused, decreased response, abnormal vitals
severity_assessment.severe: Unresponsive, GCS < 8, airway compromise

diagnosis_name: Altered Sensorium with Suspected Raised Intracranial Pressure

first_line_drugs.notes: Maintain SpO2 >95%, via mask/HFNC/ventilator as required
10–20 ml/kg IV bolus in shock, then maintenance; avoid hypotonic fluids
5 ml/kg IV bolus over 10–15 min, then 0.1–1 ml/kg/hr infusion for 48–72 hrs (monitor Na <155 mEq/L)
1–5 ml/kg IV over 20 min, repeat every 4–6 hr SOS; monitor serum osmolality <320 mOsm
2–4 ml/kg IV bolus if hypoglycemia present or suspected
20–40 mg/kg IV loading, then 10–20 mg/kg/day divided doses

second_line_drugs.notes: 50–100 mg/kg/day IV divided BD (empirical for meningitis/encephalitis)
10 mg/kg IV every 8 hr if HSV encephalitis suspected
20 mg PE/kg IV loading if seizures not controlled

investigations.name: Serum Electrolytes, Urea, Creatinine, LFT, Ammonia
CT/MRI Brain
investigations.indication: Focal deficit, unequal pupils, suspected raised ICP

investigations.name: Lumbar Puncture
investigations.indication: Suspected meningitis after stabilization (GCS >12 or stable)

counselling: Child is critically ill and requires ICU monitoring — बच्चा गंभीर रूप से बीमार है और ICU में निगरानी जरूरी है
Avoid giving anything orally if child is drowsy — अगर बच्चा सुस्त है तो मुंह से कुछ न दें
Strict adherence to medications is important — दवाइयाँ समय पर और नियमित रूप से देना बहुत जरूरी है
Keep child in calm, low-stimulation environment — बच्चे को शांत माहौल में रखें
Immediate reporting if condition worsens — हालत बिगड़ने पर तुरंत डॉक्टर को बताएं
warning_signs: Fast breathing / chest indrawing
Bluish lips or tongue
Convulsions / fits
Excessive sleepiness / unresponsive
Persistent vomiting
Unequal pupils or abnormal posturing
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/neurologist if GCS <12, seizures, focal deficits, or suspected raised ICP
hospitalisation_criteria: GCS <12, seizures, hypoxia, shock, raised ICP signs, inability to maintain airway
notes: Always treat ABC first. Assume raised ICP in all altered sensorium. Avoid overzealous hyperventilation and hypotonic fluids.
source: IAP Pediatric Emergency Guidelines, WHO 2023, Pediatric Neurocritical Care protocols
guideline_changes: Hypertonic saline increasingly preferred over mannitol in pediatric raised ICP
expected_course: Stabilization within 24–72 hrs; recovery depends on etiology (days to weeks)
key_clinical_points: ABC stabilization is the first priority
Hypoglycemia must be corrected immediately
Every altered sensorium patient should be treated as raised ICP until proven otherwise

severity_assessment.mild: Drowsy but arousable, normal vitals
severity_assessment.moderate: Confused, decreased response, abnormal vitals
severity_assessment.severe: GCS <8, unresponsive, airway compromise

first_line_drugs.notes: High-flow oxygen immediately; maintain SpO2 >94%
Fluid bolus 20 mL/kg over 5–10 min; repeat up to 40–60 mL/kg with reassessment; stop if hepatomegaly, rales, or fluid overload
Start within 1 hour of recognition of septic shock; choice based on local protocol
0.05–0.3 mcg/kg/min IV for cold shock (first-line inotrope)
Start at 0.05 mcg/kg/min and titrate upward for warm shock

second_line_drugs.notes: 5–10 mcg/kg/min if epinephrine not available
Used in myocardial dysfunction with low cardiac output
Indicated in catecholamine-resistant shock or suspected adrenal insufficiency
Add in refractory warm shock not responding to norepinephrine

counselling: Septic shock is a life-threatening emergency requiring ICU care — सेप्टिक शॉक एक जानलेवा आपातकालीन स्थिति है जिसमें ICU में इलाज जरूरी है
Immediate treatment with fluids and antibiotics is essential — तुरंत तरल पदार्थ और एंटीबायोटिक से इलाज बहुत जरूरी है
Continuous monitoring is required — लगातार निगरानी जरूरी है
Condition can worsen rapidly if untreated — इलाज न होने पर हालत तेजी से बिगड़ सकती है
Follow-up and prolonged treatment may be required — फॉलो-अप और लंबे समय तक इलाज की जरूरत हो सकती है
warning_signs: Altered consciousness
Cold extremities or delayed capillary refill
Low blood pressure
Rapid breathing
Reduced urine output
Seizures
referral_criteria: Immediate referral to PICU/intensivist
hospitalisation_criteria: All septic shock cases require ICU admission
notes: Follow ABC approach. Early fluid resuscitation and antibiotics are lifesaving. Identify cold vs warm shock to guide inotrope selection.
guideline_changes: Early epinephrine preferred over dopamine in pediatric septic shock
expected_course: Improvement depends on early resuscitation; stabilization usually within 24–72 hours if treated promptly
key_clinical_points: Give antibiotics within 1 hour
Fluid bolus 20 mL/kg rapidly
Start inotropes early if fluid refractory

severity_assessment.mild: Compensated shock with normal BP but poor perfusion
severity_assessment.moderate: Hypotension with tachycardia and poor perfusion
severity_assessment.severe: Refractory shock requiring multiple inotropes

diagnosis_name: Stress Hyperglycemia in Critically Ill Child

first_line_drugs.notes: Start 0.02–0.05 units/kg/hr IV infusion; titrate to maintain RBS 80–180 mg/dL
Use for dilution of insulin infusion (e.g., 1 unit/ml concentration); maintain IV fluids as per requirement

second_line_drugs.notes: Add if glucose falls <100 mg/dL or to maintain GIR in metabolic conditions

investigations.name: Blood Gas (ABG)
investigations.indication: To rule out acidosis (DKA vs stress hyperglycemia)

counselling: High blood sugar is common in critically ill children — गंभीर बीमार बच्चों में शुगर बढ़ना आम है
Insulin infusion will be used to control glucose safely — इंसुलिन इन्फ्यूजन से शुगर को सुरक्षित तरीके से नियंत्रित किया जाएगा
Frequent monitoring of sugar levels is required — बार-बार शुगर की जांच जरूरी है
Do not stop feeding unless advised — जब तक डॉक्टर न कहें, खाना-पीना बंद न करें
Condition will be closely monitored in ICU — ICU में हालत की करीबी निगरानी की जाएगी
warning_signs: Very high glucose (>300–400 mg/dL)
Dehydration or poor perfusion
Altered sensorium
Acidosis (deep breathing)
Seizures
Hypoglycemia during treatment
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/endocrinologist if persistent hyperglycemia or suspected DKA
hospitalisation_criteria: All critically ill children with hyperglycemia requiring insulin infusion
notes: Do not reduce glucose infusion rate in metabolic conditions; instead add insulin to prevent catabolism
guideline_changes: Target glucose range relaxed to 80–180 mg/dL to avoid hypoglycemia risk
expected_course: Glucose stabilizes within 24–48 hrs with proper insulin titration
key_clinical_points: Start insulin if RBS >180 mg/dL
Avoid tight glycemic control to prevent hypoglycemia
Continue nutritional support (do not reduce GIR unnecessarily)

severity_assessment.mild: RBS 180–250 mg/dL, no symptoms
severity_assessment.moderate: RBS 250–400 mg/dL, mild dehydration
severity_assessment.severe: RBS >400 mg/dL or associated with acidosis/altered sensorium

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Hourly for first 4 hours, then 2–4 hourly

diagnosis_name: Electrolyte Imbalance with Laboratory Corrections (Corrected Calcium, Sodium, Osmolality)

first_line_drugs.notes: Management depends on primary abnormality (e.g., hypocalcemia, hyponatremia, hyperglycemia, DKA). Use corrected values to guide therapy rather than measured values alone.
Use isotonic fluids initially; adjust based on corrected sodium and osmolality values

second_line_drugs.notes: Calcium, sodium, potassium correction guided by corrected lab values and clinical condition

investigations.name: Serum Calcium
investigations.indication: To calculate corrected calcium in hypoalbuminemia

investigations.name: Serum Sodium
investigations.indication: To calculate corrected sodium in hyperglycemia

counselling: Lab values may need correction based on albumin or glucose levels — जांच के नंबर एल्ब्युमिन या शुगर के अनुसार ठीक किए जा सकते हैं
Treatment decisions are based on corrected values, not raw reports — इलाज सही किए गए नंबरों पर आधारित होता है, सिर्फ रिपोर्ट पर नहीं
Do not panic with abnormal numbers without clinical correlation — असामान्य नंबर देखकर घबराएं नहीं, डॉक्टर से सलाह लें
Regular monitoring is required for accurate treatment — सही इलाज के लिए नियमित जांच जरूरी है
Follow doctor advice strictly for fluid and electrolyte correction — तरल पदार्थ और इलेक्ट्रोलाइट सुधार के लिए डॉक्टर की सलाह का सख्ती से पालन करें
warning_signs: Altered consciousness
Seizures
Severe dehydration
Arrhythmias
Persistent vomiting
Weakness or paralysis
referral_criteria: Refer if severe electrolyte imbalance, altered sensorium, or complex metabolic disturbance
hospitalisation_criteria: Admit if symptomatic electrolyte imbalance or abnormal osmolality requiring IV correction
notes: Important formulae: Corrected Calcium = Total Ca + 0.8(4 - albumin). Corrected Na = Measured Na + 1.6[(glucose - 100)/100]. Osmolality = 2Na + glucose/18 + BUN/2.8.
expected_course: Electrolyte correction depends on underlying cause; lab values normalize gradually with treatment
key_clinical_points: Always use corrected calcium in hypoalbuminemia
Correct sodium in hyperglycemia before interpreting hyponatremia
Osmolality helps assess severity of metabolic derangement

severity_assessment.mild: Minor lab abnormalities without symptoms
severity_assessment.moderate: Lab abnormalities with mild clinical symptoms
severity_assessment.severe: Significant lab derangement with neurological or cardiac symptoms

counselling: Burping is helpful but not always necessary — डकार दिलाना फ़ायदेमंद है लेकिन हमेशा ज़रूरी नहीं
Do not wake a sleeping, comfortable baby for burping — सोते हुए आराम से लेटे बच्चे को डकार के लिए न जगाएं
Multiple techniques can be tried safely — अलग-अलग तरीके आज़माए जा सकते हैं
Avoid overfeeding and rapid feeding — ज़्यादा दूध या जल्दी-जल्दी दूध न पिलाएं
Seek medical advice if warning signs appear — खतरे के लक्षण दिखें तो डॉक्टर से मिलें
warning_signs: Projectile or bilious (green) vomiting — उल्टी में हरा रंग या ज़ोर से उल्टी
Persistent abdominal distension — पेट का लगातार फूला रहना
Poor feeding or weight loss — दूध कम पीना या वज़न घटना
Persistent irritability or back arching — लगातार चिड़चिड़ापन या पीठ का तनना
Blood in vomit or stool — उल्टी या मल में खून
expected_course: Most neonates do not require active burping if they are comfortable after feeds. Mild spit-up is normal and resolves by 4-6 months of age. Burping techniques help reduce discomfort from swallowed air, especially in bottle-fed babies.
key_clinical_points: Burping is a supportive measure, not a medical treatment
Not all babies need burping after every feed
Bottle-fed babies swallow more air and benefit more from burping
If baby is comfortable and gaining weight, no intervention needed
Persistent vomiting or poor weight gain warrants further evaluation

diagnosis_name: Safe Infant Transport and Car Seat Counseling
counselling: Always use a car seat, even for short trips — छोटी यात्रा में भी कार सीट ज़रूर लगाएं
Rear-facing is safest until at least 2 years or weight/height limit — पीछे की ओर मुंह करके बैठाना 2 साल तक सबसे सुरक्षित है
Ensure snug harness and proper clip position — बेल्ट कसी हो और क्लिप सही जगह हो
Never leave baby unattended in car seat outside vehicle — गाड़ी के बाहर कार सीट में बच्चे को अकेला न छोड़ें
Avoid aftermarket accessories unless approved — बिना मान्यता वाले एक्सेसरीज़ का इस्तेमाल न करें
Check expiry date of car seat — कार सीट की एक्सपायरी डेट जांचें
warning_signs: Infant shows color change or breathing difficulty in car seat — कार सीट में बच्चे का रंग बदलना या सांस लेने में तकलीफ़
Head slumping forward or chin on chest — सिर आगे गिरना या ठोड़ी छाती पर
Apnea or desaturation episodes — सांस रुकना या ऑक्सीजन कम होना
Sweating or excessive irritability — ज़्यादा पसीना या चिड़चिड़ापन
expected_course: Counseling is provided at discharge and reinforced at every OPD visit. Most families adapt well with demonstration. Preterm and low-birth-weight infants require a car seat challenge test before discharge to ensure safe tolerance of the semi-upright position.
key_clinical_points: Indian context: car seat use is low but injury prevention counseling is essential
Preterm infants (<37 weeks) and LBW (<2.5 kg) need car seat challenge test
Car seat should NOT be used as a routine sleep device at home
Rear-facing position is mandatory until at least 2 years of age
Avoid bulky clothing under harness straps

diagnosis_name: Tummy Time Guidance for Newborns and Infants
counselling: Tummy time is safe when supervised — निगरानी में पेट के बल लेटना सुरक्षित है
Initial fussiness is normal; gradual introduction helps — शुरू में रोना सामान्य है; धीरे-धीरे आदत बनाएं
Consistency is key for development — नियमितता विकास के लिए ज़रूरी है
Helps prevent flat head and supports early milestones — सिर चपटा होने से बचाता है और विकास में मदद करता है
Make tummy time interactive and enjoyable — खेल-खेल में करें, मज़ेदार बनाएं
Avoid forcing if baby is very distressed — बहुत रोए तो ज़बरदस्ती न करें
warning_signs: No attempt to lift head by 2 months — 2 महीने तक सिर उठाने की कोशिश न करना
Persistent head lag beyond 3-4 months — 3-4 महीने बाद भी सिर पीछे गिरना
Asymmetrical movement or head preference — एक तरफ़ सिर रखने की आदत या असमान हलचल
Excessive stiffness or floppiness — ज़्यादा अकड़न या ढीलापन
Delayed motor milestones — विकास के पड़ाव देर से पूरे होना
expected_course: Start tummy time from first few days of life with short 1-2 minute sessions. By 3 months, aim for 30-60 minutes total per day in divided sessions. Most babies become comfortable with tummy time within 2-4 weeks of regular practice. Head control improves progressively and supports rolling, crawling, and sitting milestones.
key_clinical_points: Tummy time is ONLY during supervised waking hours — never during sleep
Back sleeping is always recommended for sleep (reduces SIDS risk)
Can begin on parent's chest in first few weeks if baby is stable
Positional plagiocephaly prevention starts with early tummy time
Screen for torticollis if persistent head turn preference is noted
Premature babies may need modified approach based on medical stability

counselling: Cord will dry and fall off on its own; do not pull it — नाल सूखकर खुद गिर जाएगी; खींचें नहीं
Keep the area clean and dry at all times — जगह को हमेशा साफ़ और सूखा रखें
Fold diaper below the stump to avoid moisture — डायपर को नाल के नीचे मोड़कर रखें
Avoid any home remedies or powders — कोई घरेलू उपचार या पाउडर न लगाएं
Mild discharge or bleeding after fall is normal — नाल गिरने के बाद हल्का रिसाव या खून सामान्य है
Seek medical help if redness, pus, foul smell, fever, or excessive crying occurs — लालिमा, पीप, बदबू, बुखार या ज़्यादा रोना हो तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Redness extending beyond base of stump — नाल के आस-पास लालिमा फैलना
Purulent or foul-smelling discharge — पीप या बदबूदार रिसाव
Fever, lethargy, poor feeding — बुखार, सुस्ती, दूध न पीना
Persistent bleeding — लगातार खून आना
Cord not falling off by 3-4 weeks — 3-4 हफ़्ते में नाल न गिरना
expected_course: The umbilical cord stump dries and separates naturally within 5-15 days. Color changes from white to yellow to brown/black before falling off. Mild serous ooze after separation is normal. Delayed separation beyond 3 weeks may indicate infection or immune deficiency and warrants evaluation.
key_clinical_points: Dry cord care is the standard of care (WHO recommendation)
4% chlorhexidine application recommended only in high-risk/community settings
Traditional practices (oil, turmeric, ghee, ash) increase infection risk
Delayed separation (>3 weeks) may indicate leukocyte adhesion deficiency
Umbilical granuloma (persistent red nodule) treated with silver nitrate cauterization
Omphalitis signs require urgent referral and IV antibiotics

severity_assessment.mild: Normal drying cord, no discharge, no redness — dry cord care, reassurance
severity_assessment.moderate: Minor serous ooze, mild periumbilical redness, delayed separation — close follow-up, chlorhexidine if needed
severity_assessment.severe: Purulent discharge, spreading erythema, systemic signs (fever, lethargy) — urgent referral for omphalitis management

counselling: Delay the first bath; vernix has protective benefits — पहला नहाना देर से करें; वर्निक्स (सफेद परत) बच्चे की त्वचा की रक्षा करती है
Sponge bath is enough until cord falls off — नाल गिरने तक स्पंज से पोंछना काफी है
Avoid over-bathing; 2–3 baths per week are sufficient — ज़्यादा नहलाने से बचें; हफ्ते में 2–3 बार काफी है
Daily cleaning of face, neck folds, and diaper area is more important than frequent full baths — चेहरे, गर्दन की सिलवटों और डायपर वाली जगह की रोज़ सफ़ाई, बार-बार नहलाने से ज़्यादा ज़रूरी है
Always support the baby's head and neck during bath — नहलाते समय बच्चे के सिर और गर्दन को हमेशा सहारा दें
Keep baby warm before, during, and after the bath — नहलाने से पहले, बीच में और बाद में बच्चे को गर्म रखें
Seek medical advice if baby becomes cold, blue, excessively sleepy, or develops skin irritation — अगर बच्चा ठंडा, नीला, ज़्यादा सुस्त हो जाए या त्वचा पर जलन हो तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Baby turns blue or pale during or after bath — नहाते समय या बाद में बच्चा नीला या पीला पड़ जाए
Baby feels cold to touch after bath — नहाने के बाद बच्चा छूने में ठंडा लगे
Excessive crying that does not settle — बहुत ज़्यादा रोना जो शांत न हो
Skin rash, redness or peeling after bath — नहाने के बाद त्वचा पर दाने, लालिमा या छिलना
Vomiting or drowsiness after bath — नहाने के बाद उल्टी या सुस्ती
Cord area becomes red, smelly or has pus — नाल वाली जगह लाल हो जाए, बदबू आए या मवाद निकले
expected_course: Newborns generally tolerate bathing well once stable and thermoregulated. The first bath can be safely given after 24 hours of life. Sponge baths are recommended until the umbilical cord falls off (usually 7–14 days). Skin dryness and mild peeling in the first 2 weeks is normal and resolves spontaneously.
key_clinical_points: WHO recommends delaying the first bath to at least 24 hours after birth
Vernix caseosa is protective — do not scrub it off
Hypothermia risk is highest in preterm and LBW babies during bathing
Room temperature should be at least 25°C during bath
Water temperature 37°C — test with elbow or thermometer
Sponge bath until umbilical cord separates
Avoid talcum powder — aspiration risk
No soap needed for neonates; plain warm water is sufficient

counselling: Gestational age tells us how mature your baby is — गर्भकालीन आयु बताती है कि आपका बच्चा कितना परिपक्व है
Correct gestational age matters for feeding, warmth, screening, and monitoring — सही गर्भकालीन आयु दूध पिलाने, गर्मी, जांच और निगरानी के लिए ज़रूरी है
Prematurity does not always mean low birth weight alone — समय से पहले जन्म का मतलब सिर्फ़ कम वज़न नहीं है
Mild prematurity is manageable with proper follow-up — हल्की अपरिपक्वता सही देखभाल से ठीक हो जाती है
Closer monitoring may be needed if baby appears less mature — अगर बच्चा कम परिपक्व दिखे तो ज़्यादा निगरानी की ज़रूरत हो सकती है
warning_signs: Poor feeding or weak suck — कम दूध पीना या कमज़ोर चूसना
Temperature instability (feeling cold or hot) — तापमान अस्थिरता (ठंडा या गर्म लगना)
Breathing difficulty or apnea — सांस लेने में तकलीफ़ या सांस रुकना
Excessive jaundice — बहुत ज़्यादा पीलिया
Poor weight gain — वज़न न बढ़ना
Lethargy or irritability — सुस्ती या चिड़चिड़ापन
expected_course: Term infants (37-42 weeks) follow standard growth trajectories. Late preterm infants (34-36 weeks) may have feeding difficulties and temperature instability for the first 1-2 weeks but generally catch up by 4-6 weeks. Very preterm infants (<34 weeks) require specialized care. Growth and development milestones should be assessed using corrected age for preterm infants until 2 years.
key_clinical_points: New Ballard Score is the standard clinical tool for GA assessment (valid 20-44 weeks)
Best performed within first 4 days of life
Six neuromuscular + six physical maturity criteria scored
Early ultrasound (first trimester) is the most accurate method for GA dating
Classify as SGA/AGA/LGA based on weight-for-GA centile
Use corrected age for growth and development in preterms
Use chronological age for vaccination scheduling

monitoring_parameters.frequency: At birth, daily if admitted, then weekly for first month
At birth and at each visit
Every 4-6 hours for late preterm; at each visit for term
At each feed for first week; then at each visit
If preterm, SGA, LGA, or IDM — per protocol

diagnosis_name: Red Flag Signs in the Neonatal OPD
counselling: Teach parents to recognize red flag signs — माता-पिता को खतरे के निशान पहचानना सिखाएं।
Explain urgency of seeking care immediately if warning signs appear — खतरे के निशान दिखें तो तुरंत डॉक्टर के पास जाएं।
Provide printed or digital list of warning signs — खतरे के निशानों की सूची लिखकर या फ़ोन में दें।
Reassure that early visits are encouraged even if unsure — अगर शक हो तो भी बच्चे को जल्दी दिखाना सही है।
Keep hospital emergency numbers saved in phone — अस्पताल के इमरजेंसी नंबर फ़ोन में सेव रखें।
warning_signs: Poor feeding or refusal to feed — दूध न पीना या मना करना
Fever (>=38 degrees C) or hypothermia (<36.5 degrees C) — बुखार या शरीर ठंडा पड़ना
Fast breathing or chest retractions — तेज़ सांस या छाती धंसना
Blue discoloration of lips or body — होंठ या शरीर का नीला पड़ना
Seizures or abnormal movements — दौरे या असामान्य हरकत
Lethargy or excessive sleepiness — सुस्ती या बहुत ज़्यादा सोना
Bilious (green) vomiting — हरी उल्टी
Abdominal distension — पेट फूलना
Blood in stools — पोटी में खून
Bulging fontanel — तालू का उभरना
expected_course: Red flag signs in a neonate indicate potential life-threatening conditions requiring immediate evaluation and stabilization. Neonates can deteriorate rapidly. Early recognition and prompt action (ABC stabilization, referral, admission) can be lifesaving.
key_clinical_points: Any fever >=38 degrees C in a neonate is a red flag requiring sepsis evaluation.
Bilious vomiting in a neonate is a surgical emergency until proven otherwise.
Neonates may present with subtle signs — lethargy, poor feeding, or temperature instability may be the only clues.
Use the FLAIR-CHiPS mnemonic: Feeding/Fever, Lethargy, Apnea, Irritability, Respiratory distress, Cyanosis, Hypothermia, Persistent jaundice, Seizures.
Never delay referral or admission if a life-threatening red flag is identified.
Document all red flags thoroughly for continuity of care.

severity_assessment.mild: No red flags present, routine OPD visit — continue routine care, counsel parents on warning signs.
severity_assessment.moderate: Single red flag, baby appears stable — thorough evaluation, investigations, close monitoring, consider admission.
severity_assessment.severe: Multiple red flags, baby appears sick or unstable — immediate ABC stabilization, admission, urgent referral.

monitoring_parameters.frequency: At each visit; continuous if admitted

counselling: Heel prick is safe and essential for early detection of serious diseases — एड़ी की चुभन सुरक्षित है और गंभीर बीमारियों की जल्दी पहचान के लिए ज़रूरी है
Pain is minimal and short-lasting — दर्द बहुत कम और थोड़ी देर का होता है
Proper technique prevents complications — सही तरीके से करने पर कोई समस्या नहीं होती
Early screening helps prevent long-term disability — जल्दी जांच से लंबी अक्षमता रोकी जा सकती है
Report any swelling, redness, or persistent bleeding — अगर सूजन, लालिमा या लगातार खून बहे तो बताएं
Breastfeeding during procedure reduces pain — जांच के दौरान दूध पिलाने से दर्द कम होता है
warning_signs: Persistent bleeding from puncture site — चुभन की जगह से लगातार खून बहना
Swelling or redness suggesting infection — संक्रमण की ओर इशारा करती सूजन या लालिमा
Excessive crying or irritability — बहुत ज़्यादा रोना या चिड़चिड़ापन
Signs of poor perfusion or lethargy — कमज़ोर रक्त प्रवाह या सुस्ती के संकेत
expected_course: The heel prick site heals within 1-2 days. Mild redness at the puncture site is normal and resolves within hours. Results of newborn screening tests are typically available within 1-2 weeks. Abnormal or borderline results require repeat testing.
key_clinical_points: Optimal timing: 48-72 hours after birth (after adequate feeding)
Use medial or lateral plantar surface of heel only — avoid center (bone injury risk)
Warm the heel for 3-5 minutes before puncture to improve blood flow
Puncture depth must not exceed 2 mm
Wipe away first drop — collect subsequent drops
Avoid excessive squeezing — causes hemolysis and false results
Non-pharmacological pain relief: skin-to-skin, sucrose, breastfeeding during procedure
Screens for: CH, CAH, PKU, G6PD, sickle cell disease, galactosemia

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Check at 1 hour and next feed for bleeding or hematoma
Review within 1-2 weeks of sample collection
If inadequate sample, <24 hours sample, or borderline results

counselling: Explain that newborn metabolic screening is preventive, not diagnostic. — नवजात की मेटाबोलिक जांच बीमारी पहचानने के लिए नहीं, बचाव के लिए है।
Discuss that false-positive results can occur and are clarified by confirmatory testing. — कभी-कभी गलत रिपोर्ट आ सकती है, जो दोबारा जांच से साफ़ हो जाती है।
Emphasize that early detection significantly improves long-term outcomes. — जल्दी पता लगने से बच्चे का भविष्य बहुत बेहतर होता है।
If screened too early, repeat testing may be needed. — अगर जन्म के तुरंत बाद जांच हुई तो दोबारा करानी पड़ सकती है।
Heel prick test is safe and causes only brief discomfort. — एड़ी से खून लेना सुरक्षित है और थोड़ी ही देर तकलीफ़ होती है।
warning_signs: Poor feeding or vomiting — दूध न पीना या उल्टी
Excessive sleepiness or lethargy — बहुत ज़्यादा सोना या सुस्ती
Seizures or abnormal movements — दौरे या असामान्य हरकत
Unusual body odor — शरीर से अजीब गंध
Failure to gain weight — वज़न न बढ़ना
Jaundice that is worsening — पीलिया बढ़ना
expected_course: Normal screening results require no further action. Borderline results need repeat testing. Abnormal or positive results require urgent confirmatory diagnostic testing and disease-specific treatment. With early detection and treatment, many metabolic disorders have excellent outcomes.
key_clinical_points: Ideal sample timing is 24-72 hours after birth.
Samples collected before 24 hours may give false negatives and must be repeated.
Preterm babies, sick neonates, and transfused babies require repeat screening.
Congenital hypothyroidism is the most common condition detected — early levothyroxine prevents intellectual disability.
G6PD deficiency screening helps prevent hemolytic crises with trigger avoidance.
Parents must be counseled to keep results safely and follow up promptly.

counselling: Repeat testing ensures accurate diagnosis — दोबारा जांच से सही पहचान होती है।
Repeat does not always indicate disease — दोबारा जांच का मतलब बीमारी नहीं है।
Early detection improves outcomes — जल्दी पहचान से इलाज बेहतर होता है।
Strict follow-up is essential — समय पर जांच कराना बहुत ज़रूरी है।
Keep all reports safely for every visit — सभी रिपोर्ट संभालकर रखें और हर बार लाएं।
warning_signs: Poor feeding or vomiting — दूध न पीना या उल्टी
Lethargy or excessive sleepiness — सुस्ती या बहुत ज़्यादा सोना
Seizures — दौरे पड़ना
Jaundice worsening — पीलिया बढ़ना
Failure to gain weight — वज़न न बढ़ना
Unusual body odor — शरीर से अजीब गंध
expected_course: Repeat screening is required when the initial sample was collected too early, was inadequate, or when the baby has risk factors (prematurity, NICU stay, blood transfusion). Most repeat screens are normal. Abnormal results require urgent confirmatory testing and specialist referral.
key_clinical_points: Sample collected before 24 hours must be repeated at 48-72 hours.
NICU babies need repeat screening at discharge or day 7-14.
Post-transfusion babies require repeat screening after 72 hours.
Preterm infants (<32 weeks) need repeat at term corrected age.
Poor or inadequate samples need immediate recollection.
Failure to follow up abnormal results is the most dangerous gap in screening programs.

counselling: Screening is safe, painless, and quick — जांच सुरक्षित, दर्दरहित और जल्दी होती है।
Failure does not always mean permanent hearing loss — फेल होने का मतलब हमेशा बहरापन नहीं है।
Follow-up is essential — फॉलो-अप ज़रूरी है।
Early detection improves speech and language development — जल्दी पहचान से बोलने और भाषा के विकास में सुधार होता है।
The 1-2-3 rule: Screen by 1 month, diagnose by 2 months, intervene by 3 months — 1-2-3 नियम: 1 महीने तक जांच, 2 महीने तक पहचान, 3 महीने तक इलाज शुरू।
warning_signs: No startle to loud sound by 1 month — 1 महीने तक तेज़ आवाज़ पर न चौंकना
No babbling by 6 months — 6 महीने तक बोलने की कोशिश न करना
No response to name by 9 months — 9 महीने तक नाम पर प्रतिक्रिया न देना
No words by 12 months — 12 महीने तक कोई शब्द न बोलना
Parent concern about hearing — माता-पिता को सुनने में संदेह
expected_course: Most newborns pass the initial hearing screening. Those who fail require rescreening within 2-4 weeks. Persistent failure leads to diagnostic audiology by 2 months of age. With confirmed hearing loss, intervention (hearing aids, cochlear implant evaluation) should begin by 3 months to optimize speech and language outcomes.
key_clinical_points: Universal newborn hearing screening (UNHS) is recommended for all newborns.
OAE screens cochlear function; AABR assesses the auditory nerve pathway.
AABR is preferred for NICU babies (risk of auditory neuropathy).
High-risk babies need periodic monitoring even if initial screen is normal.
Follow the 1-2-3 rule: Screen by 1 month, Diagnose by 2 months, Intervene by 3 months.

counselling: Breastfeeding is a learned skill that improves with time — स्तनपान एक सीखने वाली कला है जो समय के साथ बेहतर होती है
Feed on demand and watch baby cues — बच्चे की ज़रूरत के अनुसार दूध पिलाएं और उसके संकेत देखें
Avoid pacifiers in first 4-6 weeks — पहले 4-6 हफ़्ते चुसनी न दें
Seek help early if pain or poor feeding — अगर दर्द हो या बच्चा ठीक से दूध न पिए तो जल्दी मदद लें
Exclusive breastfeeding recommended for first 6 months — पहले 6 महीने केवल माँ का दूध पिलाएं
warning_signs: Baby not waking for feeds for more than 4 hours — बच्चा 4 घंटे से ज़्यादा दूध के लिए न जागे
Less than 6 wet diapers in 24 hours after day 4 — चौथे दिन के बाद 24 घंटे में 6 से कम गीले डायपर
Poor weight gain or weight loss after day 5 — पांचवें दिन के बाद वज़न न बढ़ना या घटना
Persistent clicking sounds during feeding — दूध पीते समय लगातार क्लिक की आवाज़
Cracked or bleeding nipples — फटे या खून निकलते निप्पल
Baby appears jaundiced or lethargic — बच्चा पीला या सुस्त दिखे
expected_course: Most mothers and babies establish effective breastfeeding by day 3-5 of life. Initial breast engorgement peaks around day 3-4 and resolves within 24-48 hours. Weight loss of up to 7-10% in first 3-5 days is physiological. Birth weight is typically regained by day 10-14. Mature milk comes in by day 3-5.
key_clinical_points: Colostrum (first milk) is adequate for the first 2-3 days — no supplements needed
Proper latch: wide open mouth, chin touching breast, nose free, lower areola in mouth
Feeding frequency: 8-12 times in 24 hours in the first few weeks
Cross-cradle hold is best for learning; football hold for C-section mothers
Signs of adequate intake: audible swallowing, relaxed hands, satisfied baby after feed
Weight loss >10% or no regain by day 14 requires evaluation
Avoid prelacteal feeds (honey, ghutti, formula) — harmful and unnecessary

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Day 3-5, day 10-14, then weekly for first month
At each OPD visit in first 2 weeks

counselling: Mild redness, swelling, and yellow film can be part of normal healing — हल्की लालिमा, सूजन और पीली परत सामान्य ठीक होने का हिस्सा है
Bleeding should stop with gentle pressure — हल्का दबाव देने से खून रुक जाना चाहिए
Seek urgent review if baby does not pass urine, develops fever, or has continuous bleeding — अगर बच्चा पेशाब न करे, बुखार हो, या लगातार खून बहे तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
Do not apply home remedies, powders, or antiseptics unless advised — बिना डॉक्टर की सलाह के घरेलू उपचार, पाउडर या एंटीसेप्टिक न लगाएं
Apply petroleum jelly to prevent sticking to diaper — डायपर से चिपकने से बचाने के लिए वैसलीन लगाएं
Follow-up if healing appears delayed or abnormal — अगर ठीक होने में देरी लगे या असामान्य लगे तो दोबारा दिखाएं
warning_signs: Continuous bleeding that does not stop with pressure — लगातार खून बहना जो दबाव से न रुके
No urine output for more than 6-8 hours — 6-8 घंटे से ज़्यादा पेशाब न आना
Fever — बुखार
Pus or foul-smelling discharge — मवाद या बदबूदार रिसाव
Excessive swelling or redness — बहुत ज़्यादा सूजन या लालिमा
Baby refusing feeds or becoming lethargic — बच्चे का दूध न पीना या सुस्त हो जाना
expected_course: Normal healing after circumcision takes 7-10 days. A yellow-white film (fibrin) over the wound is normal and should not be removed. Mild swelling resolves within 3-5 days. If Plastibell is used, the ring falls off in 5-10 days. Complete healing may take 2-3 weeks.
key_clinical_points: Absolute contraindications: hypospadias, epispadias, chordee, buried penis
Delay circumcision in preterm, LBW (<2.5 kg), sick, or bleeding disorder babies
Always check for hypospadias before circumcision — foreskin needed for repair
Plastibell technique: ring falls off in 5-10 days; less bleeding
Pain management: sucrose, EMLA cream, or dorsal penile nerve block
Yellow fibrin film is normal healing — not infection
Written informed consent is mandatory

severity_assessment.mild: Normal post-procedure appearance with mild swelling and yellow film — routine care with petroleum jelly
severity_assessment.moderate: Increased redness, delayed ring separation, mild oozing — close observation, possible antibiotics
severity_assessment.severe: Continuous bleeding, no urine output, pus, fever, glans injury — emergency evaluation, possible surgical intervention

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Every diaper change for first 24 hours
At first review (24-72 hours) and second review (5-10 days)
At each feed for first 24 hours

counselling: Colic peaks at 6-8 weeks and usually resolves by 3-4 months. — कोलिक 6-8 हफ़्ते में सबसे ज़्यादा होता है और 3-4 महीने तक अपने आप ठीक हो जाता है।
The crying is not due to poor parenting, pain or hunger in most cases. — ज़्यादातर मामलों में रोना खराब परवरिश, दर्द या भूख की वजह से नहीं होता।
There are no long-term consequences for baby's health. — बच्चे की सेहत पर इसका कोई लंबे समय का असर नहीं होता।
Parents should practice self-care, share caregiving and seek support. — माता-पिता अपना ख़्याल रखें, बारी-बारी से बच्चे को संभालें और मदद लें।
Never shake the baby. — बच्चे को कभी झटके से न हिलाएं।
warning_signs: Persistent vomiting
Blood in stool
Fever or lethargy
Poor weight gain or failure to thrive
Excessive sleepiness or unresponsiveness
Abdominal distension
Refusal to feed
expected_course: Infantile colic is self-limiting. Crying episodes typically peak at 6-8 weeks of age and resolve spontaneously by 3-4 months in most infants. No long-term developmental or health consequences.
key_clinical_points: Colic is a diagnosis of exclusion; always rule out red flags first.
Reassurance and parental support are the mainstay of management.
No strong evidence supports any medication; simethicone and probiotics have limited benefit.
Never shake the baby -- educate parents about shaken baby syndrome risk during frustration.

severity_assessment.mild: Brief crying spells, infant consolable with soothing, feeding and weight gain normal.
severity_assessment.moderate: Prolonged crying >3 hours/day, difficult to console, but infant otherwise thriving and no red flags.
severity_assessment.severe: Excessive inconsolable crying with parental exhaustion, or any red flag symptom present requiring further evaluation.

recommended_tests: MRI Spine (>3 months or if US unavailable)
Pediatric Neurology / Neurosurgery referral if abnormal

counselling: Most sacral dimples are benign and harmless — अधिकतर सैक्रल डिम्पल सामान्य और हानिरहित होते हैं
Small, midline dimples with visible base usually do not need tests — छोटे, बीच में स्थित गड्ढे जिनका तल दिखता है, आमतौर पर जांच की ज़रूरत नहीं
Certain features may require imaging to rule out spinal problems — कुछ विशेषताओं में रीढ़ की समस्या जांचने के लिए स्कैन ज़रूरी हो सकता है
Observe for warning signs like limb weakness or urinary issues — पैरों की कमज़ोरी या पेशाब की समस्या जैसे लक्षणों पर ध्यान दें
Early detection ensures better outcomes — जल्दी पहचान से बेहतर इलाज होता है
warning_signs: Limb weakness or asymmetry — पैरों में कमज़ोरी या असमानता
Bladder or bowel dysfunction — पेशाब या मल त्याग में समस्या
Foot deformity — पैर की विकृति
Growing lesion or discharge from dimple — गड्ढे का बढ़ना या उसमें से रिसाव
Hair tuft or hemangioma near dimple — गड्ढे के पास बालों का गुच्छा या लाल धब्बा
expected_course: Simple sacral dimples that are small (<5 mm), midline, with visible base and no associated skin findings are benign and require no further workup. Spontaneous resolution is expected. High-risk dimples require imaging within 3 months of age (spinal ultrasound) or MRI if older.
key_clinical_points: Differentiate benign from high-risk sacral dimple at first exam
Spinal ultrasound is best done before 3 months (open acoustic window)
Distance >2.5 cm from anus increases suspicion for occult spinal dysraphism
Normal neurological exam is reassuring but does not exclude deep pathology
Document size, location, base visibility, and associated findings at each visit
